parkSubtitle: The most visited theme park in the world - home to Cinderella Castle, Space Mountain, Pirates of the Caribbean, and classic Disney magic.
parkDescription: Magic Kingdom at Walt Disney World is the most iconic theme park on Earth. Opened on October 1, 1971, it was the first park built at Walt Disney World and remains the most visited theme park in the world with over 20 million guests annually. The park spans 107 acres and is organized around six themed lands radiating from the central hub in front of Cinderella Castle.
history: Magic Kingdom was Walt Disney's original vision for a theme park that improved upon the Disneyland concept in California. Construction began in 1967, and the park utilized a revolutionary underground utilidor system - a network of tunnels beneath the park that allows cast members and supplies to move unseen by guests. The park opened with 23 attractions and has grown to over 30, with the most recent additions including TRON Lightcycle Run in Tomorrowland.

areas.name: Main Street U.S.A.
areas.description: The grand entrance to Magic Kingdom, designed to evoke small-town America at the turn of the 20th century. Features shops, bakeries, and the iconic Cinderella Castle at the far end.
areas.topRides: Walt Disney World Railroad, Main Street Vehicles

areas.name: Adventureland
areas.description: A tropical paradise inspired by exotic destinations from around the world. Home to some of Disney's most classic attractions.
areas.topRides: Pirates of the Caribbean, Jungle Cruise, Magic Carpets of Aladdin

areas.name: Frontierland
areas.description: The wild west comes alive with thrilling rides and classic Americana. Big Thunder Mountain is one of the park's most popular attractions.
areas.topRides: Big Thunder Mountain Railroad, Splash Mountain, Tom Sawyer Island

areas.name: Liberty Square
areas.description: Colonial America themed area featuring the beloved Haunted Mansion attraction and the Hall of Presidents animatronic show.
areas.topRides: Haunted Mansion, Hall of Presidents, Liberty Belle Riverboat

areas.name: Fantasyland
areas.description: The largest land in Magic Kingdom, themed around classic Disney animated films. New Fantasyland expansion added in 2012 nearly doubled the area's size.
areas.topRides: Seven Dwarfs Mine Train, Peter Pan's Flight, It's a Small World, Under the Sea

areas.name: Tomorrowland
areas.description: A retro-futuristic vision of the future featuring high-speed attractions and space-themed experiences.
areas.topRides: Space Mountain, TRON Lightcycle Run, Buzz Lightyear's Space Ranger Spin

topAttractions.name: TRON Lightcycle Run
topAttractions.description: The newest coaster at Magic Kingdom, this high-speed indoor/outdoor launch coaster reaches 59 mph

topAttractions.name: Space Mountain
topAttractions.description: Classic indoor roller coaster in the dark - a Magic Kingdom original since 1975

topAttractions.name: Seven Dwarfs Mine Train
topAttractions.description: Family-friendly coaster through the diamond mine from Snow White with innovative swinging car technology

topAttractions.name: Pirates of the Caribbean
topAttractions.description: Classic boat ride through scenes of pirate adventure - one of the last attractions Walt Disney personally helped design

topAttractions.name: Haunted Mansion
topAttractions.description: Iconic dark ride through a haunted estate with 999 happy haunts and groundbreaking special effects

insiderTips: Arrive 30-45 minutes before park opening to be at the front of the rope drop crowd
Use Lightning Lane (paid) or standby for the big rides - TRON and Seven Dwarfs Mine Train have the longest waits
The park is least crowded on weekdays during January, February, and September
Watch the fireworks from the bridge between the hub and Tomorrowland for a less crowded view
Eat lunch at 11 AM or 2 PM to avoid peak restaurant wait times
The monorail resort loop (Contemporary, Polynesian, Grand Floridian) is a free and fun way to see the resort area
bestTimes: Magic Kingdom is least crowded during mid-January through mid-February and mid-September through early October. Weekdays are always less crowded than weekends. Avoid the week between Christmas and New Year's, Spring Break weeks, and the Fourth of July - these are the busiest periods of the year.
ticketInfo: Single-day tickets start at $109 for adults and $104 for children ages 3-9 (prices vary by date). Multi-day tickets offer significant per-day savings. Park Hopper add-on allows visiting multiple parks in one day. Annual passes range from $399 to $1,399 depending on the tier.
dining: Magic Kingdom offers over 30 dining locations ranging from quick-service counter restaurants to table-service experiences. Be Our Guest Restaurant in Fantasyland is the most popular reservation and should be booked 60 days in advance. Other top picks include Liberty Tree Tavern for Thanksgiving-style meals and Skipper Canteen in Adventureland for adventurous cuisine.

parkSubtitle: A celebration of human achievement, world culture, and innovation - featuring the iconic Spaceship Earth and World Showcase.
parkDescription: EPCOT (Experimental Prototype Community of Tomorrow) is Walt Disney World's second theme park, spanning 305 acres. Originally envisioned by Walt Disney as a real working city, the park instead became a showcase of technology, innovation, and world cultures. EPCOT is divided into four neighborhoods: World Celebration, World Discovery, World Nature, and World Showcase.
history: EPCOT opened on October 1, 1982, as the second Walt Disney World theme park. Walt Disney originally envisioned EPCOT as a real, functioning city of the future where 20,000 residents would live and work. After his death in 1966, the concept was reimagined as a permanent world's fair. The park has undergone massive transformation since 2019, with new attractions like Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind and a reimagined entrance area replacing the aging Future World concept.

areas.name: World Celebration
areas.description: The central hub of EPCOT anchored by Spaceship Earth and the new CommuniCore Hall event space. This reimagined area replaced the former Future World center.
areas.topRides: Spaceship Earth, Journey of Water - Inspired by Moana

areas.name: World Discovery
areas.description: Home to EPCOT's most thrilling attractions with a focus on science, technology, and space exploration.
areas.topRides: Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind, Test Track, Mission: SPACE

areas.name: World Nature
areas.description: Celebrating the beauty and importance of the natural world through immersive attractions and stunning environments.
areas.topRides: Soarin' Around the World, Living with the Land, The Seas with Nemo and Friends

areas.name: World Showcase
areas.description: Eleven country pavilions circle a 40-acre lagoon, each featuring authentic architecture, cuisine, shopping, and cultural experiences from Mexico, Norway, China, Germany, Italy, USA, Japan, Morocco, France, United Kingdom, and Canada.
areas.topRides: Frozen Ever After (Norway), Remy's Ratatouille Adventure (France), Gran Fiesta Tour (Mexico)

topAttractions.name: Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind
topAttractions.description: The first reverse-launch coaster at any Disney park - an innovative indoor coaster that rotates 360 degrees while racing through space

topAttractions.name: Frozen Ever After
topAttractions.description: Boat ride through the kingdom of Arendelle in the Norway pavilion - one of the most popular rides at EPCOT

topAttractions.name: Test Track
topAttractions.description: Design your own virtual concept car and then test it on a high-speed outdoor track reaching 65 mph

topAttractions.name: Soarin' Around the World
topAttractions.description: Hang-gliding flight simulation over iconic world landmarks with wind, scents, and stunning visuals

topAttractions.name: Remy's Ratatouille Adventure
topAttractions.description: Trackless dark ride that shrinks you to the size of a rat and sends you scurrying through Gusteau's restaurant in the France pavilion

insiderTips: Enter through the International Gateway (back entrance near France) if staying at a nearby Disney resort to skip the main entrance crowds
EPCOT festivals (Food and Wine, Flower and Garden, Festival of the Arts, Festival of the Holidays) add dozens of food and drink booths - plan your visit around them
Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind uses a virtual queue - join at 7 AM or 1 PM sharp for a boarding group
World Showcase opens at 11 AM, two hours after the rest of the park - ride the big attractions first
Drinking Around the World (one drink per country pavilion) is a popular adult tradition but pace yourself across 11 countries
The France pavilion bakery, Les Halles Boulangerie Patisserie, serves the best pastries in all of Walt Disney World
bestTimes: EPCOT is least crowded on weekdays outside of festival periods and holidays. Mid-January through mid-February and September weekdays offer the shortest wait times. The park gets significantly busier during Food and Wine Festival (late August through November) and during spring break weeks.
ticketInfo: Single-day tickets start at $109 for adults and $104 for children ages 3-9 (date-based pricing). Multi-day tickets offer savings. Park Hopper add-on available. Annual passes from $399 to $1,399.
dining: EPCOT is widely considered the best Disney park for dining. World Showcase offers authentic cuisine from 11 countries. Top reservations include Le Cellier Steakhouse (Canada), Teppan Edo (Japan), Via Napoli (Italy), and Space 220 - a restaurant themed as a space station orbiting Earth with incredible views. During festivals, dozens of outdoor kitchens serve specialty items.

parkSubtitle: Home to Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge, Toy Story Land, and Tower of Terror - where movies and entertainment come to life.
parkDescription: Disney's Hollywood Studios brings the magic of movies, television, music, and theater to life across 135 acres. Originally opened as Disney-MGM Studios, the park has been transformed with the additions of Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge and Toy Story Land, making it one of the most popular parks at Walt Disney World.
history: Originally opened as Disney-MGM Studios on May 1, 1989, the park was conceived as a working film and television production facility combined with a theme park. Over time, actual production moved out and the park evolved into a fully themed entertainment destination. The name changed to Disney's Hollywood Studios in 2008. Major expansions include Toy Story Land (2018) and Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge (2019), which together reinvented the park and dramatically increased attendance.

areas.name: Hollywood Boulevard
areas.description: The park's grand entrance recreating the golden age of Hollywood in the 1930s and 1940s. The Chinese Theatre at the end houses Mickey and Minnie's Runaway Railway.
areas.topRides: Mickey and Minnie's Runaway Railway

areas.name: Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge
areas.description: A 14-acre immersive land set on the planet Batuu at the edge of the galaxy. Features incredible theming and two headliner attractions.
areas.topRides: Star Wars: Rise of the Resistance, Millennium Falcon: Smugglers Run

areas.name: Toy Story Land
areas.description: Shrink down to the size of a toy in Andy's backyard with colorful, family-friendly attractions.
areas.topRides: Slinky Dog Dash, Alien Swirling Saucers, Toy Story Mania!

areas.name: Sunset Boulevard
areas.description: Home to two of the park's most iconic thrill rides with a Sunset Strip Hollywood theming.
areas.topRides: Tower of Terror, Rock 'n' Roller Coaster Starring Aerosmith

topAttractions.name: Star Wars: Rise of the Resistance
topAttractions.description: Widely considered the most ambitious theme park attraction ever built - a 20-minute experience combining multiple ride systems into one epic adventure

topAttractions.name: Tower of Terror
topAttractions.description: The Twilight Zone Tower of Terror drops riders 13 stories in a haunted Hollywood hotel elevator shaft

topAttractions.name: Slinky Dog Dash
topAttractions.description: Family-friendly launched coaster themed around Slinky Dog from Toy Story - fun for all ages

topAttractions.name: Mickey and Minnie's Runaway Railway
topAttractions.description: Trackless dark ride using projection technology to put guests inside a Mickey Mouse cartoon

topAttractions.name: Rock 'n' Roller Coaster
topAttractions.description: Indoor launch coaster accelerating 0-57 mph in 2.8 seconds through a neon-lit cityscape set to Aerosmith music

insiderTips: Rise of the Resistance is best experienced first thing at rope drop or via Lightning Lane Multi Pass
Slinky Dog Dash has the longest afternoon waits - ride it before 10 AM
Oga's Cantina in Galaxy's Edge requires a reservation and serves unique themed cocktails
The Fantasmic! nighttime show is one of the best at any Disney park - arrive 60 minutes early for good seats
Tower of Terror photos are included with Memory Maker - make your best scream face
Build a custom lightsaber at Savi's Workshop ($249) - reservations open 60 days in advance and sell out quickly
bestTimes: Hollywood Studios is busiest on weekends and during holiday periods. Weekday mornings in January, February, and September offer the shortest waits. The park is smaller than Magic Kingdom or EPCOT, so it can feel crowded even on moderate days. Early mornings before 10 AM are the best time for the big rides.
ticketInfo: Single-day tickets start at $109 for adults and $104 for children (date-based pricing). Multi-day and Park Hopper options available. Annual passes from $399 to $1,399.
dining: Hollywood Studios dining highlights include Docking Bay 7 Food and Cargo in Galaxy's Edge (quick-service with unique themed dishes), the 50's Prime Time Cafe (where servers act as your relatives and scold you for not eating your vegetables), and Woody's Lunch Box in Toy Story Land. The Hollywood Brown Derby is the park's signature table-service restaurant.

parkSubtitle: Disney's wildest theme park - home to Pandora - The World of Avatar, Kilimanjaro Safaris, and Expedition Everest.
parkDescription: Disney's Animal Kingdom is the largest theme park at Walt Disney World at 580 acres, making it one of the largest theme parks in the world. Opened in 1998, the park combines traditional theme park rides with a zoological experience featuring over 2,000 animals from 300 species. The iconic Tree of Life stands 145 feet tall at the center of the park.
history: Disney's Animal Kingdom opened on April 22, 1998 (Earth Day), as the fourth Walt Disney World theme park. It was the brainchild of Imagineer Joe Rohde, who spent years researching and designing the park to authentically represent wildlife and conservation. The park's most transformative addition came in 2017 with the opening of Pandora - The World of Avatar, which brought the park into the nighttime entertainment era with bioluminescent lighting effects.

areas.name: Discovery Island
areas.description: The central hub of the park surrounding the Tree of Life, a 145-foot tall sculpted tree with over 300 animal carvings. It's Tough to be a Bug! 3D show is located inside the tree.
areas.topRides: It's Tough to be a Bug!

areas.name: Pandora - The World of Avatar
areas.description: A 12-acre immersive land bringing the moon of Pandora from James Cameron's Avatar to life with floating mountains, bioluminescent plants, and two incredible attractions.
areas.topRides: Avatar Flight of Passage, Na'vi River Journey

areas.name: Africa
areas.description: Themed as the fictional village of Harambe, this area features the park's signature safari attraction and the Gorilla Falls Exploration Trail.
areas.topRides: Kilimanjaro Safaris, Gorilla Falls Exploration Trail

areas.name: Asia
areas.description: Featuring the fictional kingdom of Anandapur, this area showcases Asian wildlife and houses two major attractions.
areas.topRides: Expedition Everest, Kali River Rapids

areas.name: DinoLand U.S.A.
areas.description: A quirky area themed around a roadside dinosaur attraction with carnival games and one major thrill ride.
areas.topRides: DINOSAUR, TriceraTop Spin

topAttractions.name: Avatar Flight of Passage
topAttractions.description: A breathtaking flight simulator that puts you on the back of a banshee soaring over Pandora - consistently rated one of the best rides in the world

topAttractions.name: Kilimanjaro Safaris
topAttractions.description: An 18-minute guided safari through 110 acres of African savanna with live animals including giraffes, elephants, lions, and rhinos

topAttractions.name: Expedition Everest
topAttractions.description: High-speed roller coaster through the Himalayan mountains that includes a backwards section and an encounter with the Yeti

topAttractions.name: Na'vi River Journey
topAttractions.description: Gentle boat ride through a bioluminescent rainforest on Pandora featuring the most complex animatronic Disney has ever created

topAttractions.name: DINOSAUR
topAttractions.description: Intense dark ride through the Cretaceous period dodging dinosaurs in a time rover vehicle

insiderTips: Avatar Flight of Passage has the longest waits in all of Walt Disney World - ride it first at rope drop or use Lightning Lane
Kilimanjaro Safaris is best in the early morning when animals are most active and temperatures are cooler
Pandora at night is one of the most beautiful experiences in any theme park - the bioluminescent effects are stunning
The Maharajah Jungle Trek and Gorilla Falls Exploration Trail are free walking trails with incredible animal viewing
Festival of the Lion King is a must-see live show - arrive 30 minutes early for the best seats
The park closes earlier than other Disney parks, so plan to arrive early and maximize morning hours
bestTimes: Animal Kingdom is least crowded during weekdays in January, February, and September. The park typically opens earliest and closes earliest among the four Disney parks. Early mornings are best for both ride wait times and animal viewing on the safari.
ticketInfo: Single-day tickets start at $109 for adults and $104 for children (date-based pricing). Multi-day and Park Hopper options available. Annual passes from $399 to $1,399.
dining: Satu'li Canteen in Pandora is the park's best quick-service restaurant with creative bowls and unique Pandora-themed cocktails. Tusker House offers a character breakfast buffet with African-inspired cuisine. Yak and Yeti is the signature table-service option with Asian fusion dishes. Flame Tree Barbecue offers some of the best quick-service food in any Disney park with great waterfront seating.

parkSubtitle: Disney's tropical water park featuring one of the largest wave pools in North America and the iconic Miss Tilly shrimp boat.
parkDescription: Disney's Typhoon Lagoon is a 61-acre water park themed around a fictional tropical paradise struck by a massive typhoon. The centerpiece is the iconic Miss Tilly shrimp boat perched atop Mount Mayday, a 95-foot peak. The park features one of the largest wave pools in North America, producing 6-foot waves every 90 seconds.
parkingInfo: Free parking for water park guests
history: Typhoon Lagoon opened on June 1, 1989, as Disney's first purpose-built water park at Walt Disney World. The park's backstory tells of a powerful typhoon that struck a tropical paradise, scattering surfboards, fishing boats, and the shrimp boat Miss Tilly across the landscape. The park has received periodic updates including the addition of Miss Adventure Falls family raft ride in 2017.

areas.name: Surf Pool
areas.description: One of the largest wave pools in North America, producing 6-foot waves that roll across a massive 2.75 acre lagoon. Calm periods alternate with wave cycles.
areas.topRides: Wave Pool, Sandy White Beach

areas.name: Thrill Slides
areas.description: High-speed water slides launching from the slopes of Mount Mayday for adrenaline seekers.
areas.topRides: Crush 'n' Gusher, Humunga Kowabunga, Miss Adventure Falls

areas.name: Family Areas
areas.description: Calmer attractions perfect for families with younger children, including a lazy river and kiddie play area.
areas.topRides: Castaway Creek lazy river, Ketchakiddee Creek, Bay Slides

topAttractions.name: Crush 'n' Gusher
topAttractions.description: Water coaster with three different slide paths using jet propulsion to push riders both uphill and downhill

topAttractions.name: Humunga Kowabunga
topAttractions.description: Three speed slides that send riders down a nearly vertical 214-foot enclosed flume at up to 30 mph

topAttractions.name: Miss Adventure Falls
topAttractions.description: Family raft ride through the backstory of Captain Mary Oceaneer with treasure hunting theming

topAttractions.name: Castaway Creek
topAttractions.description: 2,100-foot lazy river winding through the entire park with caves, waterfalls, and tropical scenery

topAttractions.name: Surf Pool Waves
topAttractions.description: Massive wave pool producing 6-foot waves - one of the best wave pool experiences in the world

insiderTips: Arrive at park opening to claim a good spot - lounge chairs fill up fast on busy days
Rent a private cabana (reserve in advance) for shade, a locker, and dedicated service throughout the day
The park closes seasonally for refurbishment - check the calendar before visiting (typically closed January through mid-March)
Bring water shoes as the paths can get very hot in the Florida sun
The H2O Glow After Hours events offer exclusive nighttime access with shorter lines and glow effects
bestTimes: Typhoon Lagoon is least crowded on weekday mornings outside of summer and holiday periods. The park operates seasonally and alternates closures with Blizzard Beach. Summer weekends and holidays see the highest attendance. Arrive before 10 AM to beat the rush.
ticketInfo: Single-day tickets are $80 for adults and $74 for children ages 3-9. Annual pass add-on available. Water park tickets are separate from theme park tickets unless you purchase the Water Park and Sports option.
dining: Typhoon Lagoon offers several quick-service dining options including Leaning Palms (burgers, pizza, sandwiches), Typhoon Tilly's (fish and shrimp baskets), and Let's Go Slurpin' for frozen drinks and smoothies. No table-service restaurants are available inside the water park.

parkSubtitle: Disney's ski resort-themed water park featuring Summit Plummet - one of the tallest and fastest free-fall water slides in the world.
parkDescription: Disney's Blizzard Beach is a 66-acre water park themed around a Florida ski resort that melted in the tropical heat, turning ski slopes into water slides. The park's centerpiece is Mount Gushmore, a 90-foot peak topped with Summit Plummet, one of the tallest and fastest free-fall body slides in the world.
parkingInfo: Free parking for water park guests
history: Blizzard Beach opened on April 1, 1995, as Disney's third water park. The whimsical backstory involves a freak Florida snowstorm that inspired the construction of a ski resort. When the snow inevitably melted, the ski slopes became water slides. The park's theming includes melting snowdrifts, ski lift chairs (which serve as the actual chairlift to the top), and slalom flags along the slides.

areas.name: Mount Gushmore Summit
areas.description: The peak of the mountain with the most thrilling slides in the park including the iconic Summit Plummet.
areas.topRides: Summit Plummet, Slush Gusher, Teamboat Springs

areas.name: Green Slope
areas.description: Moderate thrill slides on the north side of the mountain offering a variety of tube and body slide experiences.
areas.topRides: Toboggan Racers, Snow Stormers, Downhill Double Dipper

areas.name: Tike's Peak
areas.description: A scaled-down version of Mount Gushmore designed specifically for young children with gentle slides and a shallow wading pool.
areas.topRides: Tike's Peak kiddie slides and play area

areas.name: Base Area
areas.description: The main gathering area with the wave pool, lazy river, and dining facilities.
areas.topRides: Melt-Away Bay wave pool, Cross Country Creek lazy river

topAttractions.name: Summit Plummet
topAttractions.description: A 120-foot near-vertical free-fall body slide reaching speeds up to 55 mph - one of the tallest in the world

topAttractions.name: Teamboat Springs
topAttractions.description: One of the longest family raft rides in the world at 1,200 feet - accommodates up to 6 riders

topAttractions.name: Slush Gusher
topAttractions.description: A 90-foot double-humped body slide that sends riders airborne over two camel-back hills

topAttractions.name: Cross Country Creek
topAttractions.description: 3,000-foot lazy river circling the entire park passing through a chilly ice cave

topAttractions.name: Downhill Double Dipper
topAttractions.description: Side-by-side racing tube slides where two riders compete head to head down the slope

insiderTips: Summit Plummet is intimidating from the top - if you are unsure, watch from the observation deck first before committing
The chairlift to the top of Mount Gushmore saves a long walk up the stairs and offers great views
Blizzard Beach alternates seasonal closures with Typhoon Lagoon - check which one is open before your trip
Lockers are available near the front entrance - rent one early as they sell out on busy days
The Ice Cream Expedition near Lottawatta Lodge serves the best frozen treats in the park
bestTimes: Blizzard Beach is least crowded on weekday mornings and during the off-season months of September and October. The park operates seasonally and alternates with Typhoon Lagoon. Summer is the busiest season. Arrive right at opening for the shortest slide wait times.
ticketInfo: Single-day tickets are $80 for adults and $74 for children ages 3-9. Annual pass add-on available. Water park tickets are separate from theme park admission.
dining: Lottawatta Lodge is the main dining venue offering burgers, hot dogs, pizza, and salads. Avalunch serves wraps and sandwiches. The Warming Hut offers funnel cakes and snacks. Frostbite Freddy's and Polar Pub serve frozen cocktails and cold beverages.

parkSubtitle: Walt Disney World's premier shopping, dining, and entertainment district - free admission with over 150 venues.
parkDescription: Disney Springs is a 120-acre outdoor shopping, dining, and entertainment complex at Walt Disney World. Formerly known as Downtown Disney, the district was completely reimagined and reopened in phases from 2013 to 2016. With over 150 shops, restaurants, and entertainment venues, Disney Springs is free to enter and offers complimentary parking.
parkingInfo: Free self-parking in garages, valet available
history: The area began as Lake Buena Vista Shopping Village in 1975, evolved into Downtown Disney Marketplace and West Side, and was fully reimagined as Disney Springs between 2013 and 2016. The $1.5 billion transformation added the Town Center section, new parking garages, and a reimagined waterfront with a cohesive story of a historic Florida spring-fed town.

areas.name: Town Center
areas.description: An open-air shopping promenade featuring premium brands like Zara, Uniqlo, Kate Spade, and Anthropologie in a Spanish-revival architectural style.
areas.topRides: N/A - shopping and dining district

areas.name: Marketplace
areas.description: The original shopping area featuring the World of Disney (one of the largest Disney merchandise stores in the world), LEGO Store, and family-friendly dining.
areas.topRides: Marketplace Carousel, Amphicar boat tours

areas.name: West Side
areas.description: Entertainment-focused area home to AMC Theatres, Splitsville Luxury Lanes bowling, Cirque du Soleil, and House of Blues.
areas.topRides: Aerophile balloon ride, AMC Dine-In Theatre

areas.name: The Landing
areas.description: Waterfront dining and nightlife area with some of Disney Springs' best restaurants including The Boathouse and Morimoto Asia.
areas.topRides: Vintage Amphicar tours, The Boathouse watercraft

topAttractions.name: World of Disney
topAttractions.description: One of the largest Disney merchandise stores in the world spanning over 50,000 square feet of shopping

topAttractions.name: The BOATHOUSE
topAttractions.description: Upscale waterfront restaurant offering steaks, seafood, and vintage Amphicar boat tours on the lake

topAttractions.name: Splitsville Luxury Lanes
topAttractions.description: Retro-modern bowling alley with 30 lanes, a full restaurant, and live entertainment

topAttractions.name: Cirque du Soleil - Drawn to Life
topAttractions.description: Disney's exclusive Cirque du Soleil show combining animation-inspired acrobatics with a touching storyline

topAttractions.name: LEGO Store
topAttractions.description: Massive LEGO retail experience with build stations, a pick-a-brick wall, and incredible LEGO sculptures throughout

insiderTips: Disney Springs is free - no admission or parking charge, making it a great option for non-park days
The best dining reservations (The Boathouse, Morimoto Asia, Wine Bar George) should be booked 60 days out
Weekday mornings are the least crowded times for shopping
The Amphicar tours at The Boathouse let you ride in a vintage 1960s car that drives into the water
Happy hour deals are available at several restaurants including Wine Bar George, Morimoto Asia, and Raglan Road
Free bus transportation runs from all Disney resort hotels to Disney Springs
bestTimes: Disney Springs is least crowded on weekday mornings and early afternoons. Friday and Saturday evenings are the busiest. Holiday shopping season (November through December) brings the largest crowds. Dining reservations are essential during peak hours.
ticketInfo: Disney Springs is completely free to visit - no admission or parking required. Individual venues, restaurants, and entertainment options have their own pricing. Cirque du Soleil tickets start at $79. Splitsville bowling starts at $17/person.
dining: Disney Springs is a dining destination in its own right. Top picks include The Boathouse (seafood and steaks), Morimoto Asia (Iron Chef Morimoto's Asian fusion), Wine Bar George (Master Sommelier George Miliotes), Raglan Road Irish Pub, Homecomin' (Chef Art Smith's Southern comfort food), and Jaleo (Jose Andres' Spanish tapas). Quick-service favorites include Blaze Pizza, The Polite Pig, and D-Luxe Burger.

parkSubtitle: A charming waterfront entertainment district inspired by 1930s Atlantic City boardwalks - dining, nightlife, and Crescent Lake views.
parkDescription: Disney's BoardWalk is a quarter-mile waterfront entertainment, dining, and shopping promenade located along Crescent Lake between EPCOT and Hollywood Studios. Inspired by the mid-Atlantic boardwalks of the 1920s and 1930s, the BoardWalk offers a nostalgic atmosphere with restaurants, bakeries, nightlife venues, and stunning water views.
parkingInfo: Limited free self-parking at BoardWalk Inn, valet available
history: Disney's BoardWalk opened on July 1, 1996, as part of the BoardWalk Inn and Villas resort. Imagineered by Robert A.M. Stern, the area was designed to capture the charm and energy of 1930s-era Atlantic City. The BoardWalk has remained a popular evening destination for guests staying at the EPCOT-area resorts, offering a scenic walking path between EPCOT's International Gateway and Hollywood Studios.

areas.name: BoardWalk Promenade
areas.description: The main waterfront walkway lined with shops, restaurants, and street performers in the evening. Perfect for a post-dinner stroll.
areas.topRides: Surrey bike rentals, Crescent Lake boat service

areas.name: Dining Row
areas.description: Several restaurants and eateries line the promenade, from quick pizza to upscale Italian dining.
areas.topRides: N/A - dining experiences

areas.name: Entertainment Zone
areas.description: Nightlife and entertainment venues including a dueling piano bar and a dance club.
areas.topRides: Jellyrolls piano bar, Atlantic Dance Hall

topAttractions.name: Jellyrolls
topAttractions.description: Lively dueling piano bar with audience participation, song requests, and a cover charge - ages 21+ only

topAttractions.name: BoardWalk Deli
topAttractions.description: Quick-service bakery and deli open late offering pastries, sandwiches, and desserts

topAttractions.name: Surrey Bike Rentals
topAttractions.description: Rent surrey bikes for a scenic ride along the waterfront around Crescent Lake

topAttractions.name: EPCOT Fireworks Viewing
topAttractions.description: Watch EPCOT's nighttime spectacular from the BoardWalk waterfront for free - a hidden gem viewing spot

topAttractions.name: Walking Path to Parks
topAttractions.description: A scenic walk to EPCOT's International Gateway (10 min) or Hollywood Studios (15 min) - no bus needed

insiderTips: The BoardWalk is free to visit for anyone - you do not need to be a resort guest
Watch EPCOT's fireworks from the BoardWalk waterfront for a less crowded, free viewing experience
Walk from the BoardWalk to EPCOT's International Gateway in about 10 minutes
Jellyrolls piano bar is one of the best-kept secrets at Disney - arrive early on weekends as it fills up
BoardWalk Bakery opens early for breakfast pastries and coffee - a great pre-park stop
Street performers entertain along the promenade in the evenings
bestTimes: The BoardWalk comes alive in the evening with street performers, live music, and waterfront dining. Weekday evenings are the most relaxed. Weekend evenings and holiday periods bring larger crowds. The area is quiet during daytime hours, making it a peaceful morning walk.
ticketInfo: Disney's BoardWalk is free to visit - no admission charge. Individual restaurants and entertainment venues have their own pricing. Jellyrolls cover charge is $15-20. Surrey bike rentals start at $25 for 30 minutes.
dining: Trattoria al Forno serves Italian breakfast and dinner with house-made pasta. The BoardWalk Deli offers quick bites and bakery items. Big River Grille and Brewing Works is a casual brewpub. For a special occasion, the recently refreshed dining options along the promenade provide waterfront views of Crescent Lake.

parkSubtitle: The original Universal Orlando theme park - home to Harry Potter's Diagon Alley, Transformers, and classic movie-themed rides.
parkDescription: Universal Studios Florida is the original theme park at Universal Orlando Resort, opened in 1990. The park brings blockbuster movies and TV shows to life across themed areas including The Wizarding World of Harry Potter - Diagon Alley, Springfield (home of The Simpsons), and New York. The park spans 108 acres and features a mix of thrill rides, 3D experiences, and live shows.
history: Universal Studios Florida opened on June 7, 1990, as the first theme park at what would become Universal Orlando Resort. The park was originally conceived as a working film studio and theme park, competing directly with Disney-MGM Studios. Major milestones include the opening of The Wizarding World of Harry Potter - Diagon Alley in 2014, which transformed the park and dramatically boosted attendance. The Hogwarts Express train connects Diagon Alley to Hogsmeade at Islands of Adventure.

areas.name: The Wizarding World of Harry Potter - Diagon Alley
areas.description: A meticulously recreated Diagon Alley from the Harry Potter films including Gringotts bank, Ollivanders wand shop, and Knockturn Alley. Enter through the London waterfront facade.
areas.topRides: Harry Potter and the Escape from Gringotts, Hogwarts Express

areas.name: Production Central
areas.description: The park entrance area featuring major attractions and an overview of movie production.
areas.topRides: Hollywood Rip Ride Rockit, Transformers: The Ride 3D

areas.name: New York
areas.description: Recreated New York City streets with brownstones, a public library, and classic architecture.
areas.topRides: Revenge of the Mummy, Race Through New York Starring Jimmy Fallon

areas.name: Springfield
areas.description: The hometown of The Simpsons brought to life with Moe's Tavern, Krusty Burger, and Lard Lad Donuts.
areas.topRides: The Simpsons Ride, Kang and Kodos' Twirl 'n' Hurl

areas.name: San Francisco / Amity
areas.description: Themed areas representing San Francisco's waterfront and the fictional town of Amity from Jaws.
areas.topRides: Fast and Furious: Supercharged

topAttractions.name: Harry Potter and the Escape from Gringotts
topAttractions.description: Multi-dimensional thrill ride combining roller coaster track with 3D projections through the vaults of Gringotts bank

topAttractions.name: Revenge of the Mummy
topAttractions.description: High-speed indoor coaster in the dark with fire effects, animatronics, and a surprising backwards launch

topAttractions.name: Hollywood Rip Ride Rockit
topAttractions.description: 17-story roller coaster where you choose your own soundtrack - features a near-vertical lift and a non-inverting loop

topAttractions.name: Transformers: The Ride 3D
topAttractions.description: Intense 3D dark ride putting you in the middle of a battle between Autobots and Decepticons

topAttractions.name: Hogwarts Express
topAttractions.description: Board the actual Hogwarts Express at King's Cross Station and ride to Hogsmeade at Islands of Adventure - requires park-to-park ticket

insiderTips: A park-to-park ticket is required to ride the Hogwarts Express between Diagon Alley and Hogsmeade
Buy a wand at Ollivanders ($55+) and use it at interactive wand spots throughout Diagon Alley - the spell locations are marked on a map
Butterbeer (frozen is the most popular version) is available throughout the Wizarding World area
Revenge of the Mummy frequently has shorter waits than you would expect for how good it is
Universal Express Pass (purchased separately) lets you skip regular lines once per ride - stays at select Universal hotels include it free
The Horror Make-Up Show is one of the funniest and most underrated attractions in the park
bestTimes: Universal Studios Florida is least crowded on weekdays from mid-January through mid-February and mid-September through October (excluding Halloween Horror Nights event nights). The park is busiest during summer, spring break, and holiday weeks. Halloween Horror Nights (September through October) requires separate tickets for evening events.
ticketInfo: Single-park tickets start at $119 for adults and $114 for children ages 3-9. Park-to-park tickets (required for Hogwarts Express) start at $174. Multi-day tickets offer savings. Universal Express Pass starts at $89/day (varies). Annual passes from $370 to $630.
dining: Diagon Alley offers themed dining at the Leaky Cauldron (British pub fare including fish and chips, bangers and mash, and Butterbeer). Springfield features Krusty Burger, Moe's Tavern (where you can order a Flaming Moe), and Lard Lad Donuts (giant pink Homer-style donuts). Lombard's Seafood Grille near the lagoon is the park's best sit-down restaurant.

parkSubtitle: Home to the Wizarding World of Harry Potter - Hogsmeade, the Incredible Hulk Coaster, and the innovative VelociCoaster.
parkDescription: Universal's Islands of Adventure is widely considered one of the best theme parks in the world. Opened in 1999, the park features eight uniquely themed islands surrounding a central lagoon. The park is known for pushing the boundaries of ride technology, from the groundbreaking Amazing Adventures of Spider-Man to the world-class VelociCoaster.
history: Islands of Adventure opened on May 28, 1999, as the second theme park at Universal Orlando. It was designed from the ground up by a team of legendary theme park designers and was immediately praised for its ambitious ride technology and immersive theming. The park's fortunes soared with the 2010 opening of The Wizarding World of Harry Potter - Hogsmeade, which fundamentally changed the Orlando theme park landscape. The 2021 opening of VelociCoaster further cemented the park's reputation for world-class attractions.

areas.name: The Wizarding World of Harry Potter - Hogsmeade
areas.description: An immersive recreation of Hogsmeade Village and Hogwarts Castle from the Harry Potter films. The snow-capped village feels like stepping into the movies.
areas.topRides: Harry Potter and the Forbidden Journey, Hagrid's Magical Creatures Motorbike Adventure, Flight of the Hippogriff

areas.name: Jurassic Park
areas.description: Themed around the Jurassic Park and Jurassic World films with lush tropical landscaping and encounters with dinosaurs.
areas.topRides: VelociCoaster, Jurassic World River Adventure, Pteranodon Flyers

areas.name: Marvel Super Hero Island
areas.description: Home to Marvel's greatest heroes with high-tech thrill rides and comic book theming.
areas.topRides: The Incredible Hulk Coaster, The Amazing Adventures of Spider-Man, Doctor Doom's Fearfall

areas.name: Seuss Landing
areas.description: A whimsical area bringing Dr. Seuss books to life with colorful architecture and family-friendly rides.
areas.topRides: The Cat in the Hat, One Fish Two Fish Red Fish Blue Fish, Caro-Seuss-el

areas.name: The Lost Continent
areas.description: Mythical themed area with Middle Eastern and ancient Greek architecture surrounding a mystical fountain.
areas.topRides: Poseidon's Fury, Mystic Fountain

areas.name: Toon Lagoon
areas.description: Classic comic strip characters come to life in this colorful area with two water rides.
areas.topRides: Dudley Do-Right's Ripsaw Falls, Popeye and Bluto's Bilge-Rat Barges

topAttractions.name: VelociCoaster
topAttractions.description: One of the best roller coasters in the world - a 155-foot, 70 mph launch coaster with four inversions and two launches through a Jurassic World raptor paddock

topAttractions.name: Hagrid's Magical Creatures Motorbike Adventure
topAttractions.description: Highly themed coaster on motorbike and sidecar vehicles through the Forbidden Forest with multiple launches and show scenes

topAttractions.name: The Incredible Hulk Coaster
topAttractions.description: Zero-to-40-mph gamma ray launch into seven inversions over the Islands of Adventure lagoon

topAttractions.name: Harry Potter and the Forbidden Journey
topAttractions.description: Innovative dark ride using KUKA robotic arms to fly guests through scenes from Harry Potter around Hogwarts Castle

topAttractions.name: The Amazing Adventures of Spider-Man
topAttractions.description: Pioneering 3D dark ride combining physical sets with high-definition projection - still one of the best dark rides ever made

insiderTips: VelociCoaster and Hagrid's have the longest waits - ride them first thing at rope drop or buy Express Pass
Hagrid's frequently experiences weather-related closures and technical delays - check the app for real-time status
Three Broomsticks in Hogsmeade serves excellent British-style food (shepherd's pie, fish and chips) and Butterbeer
The single rider line for Forbidden Journey is one of the best time-savers in any theme park
Express Pass does not work on Hagrid's or VelociCoaster - standby or early entry are your only options
If you only have one day, arrive for Early Park Admission (hotel benefit) and head straight to Hagrid's
bestTimes: Islands of Adventure is least crowded during mid-January through mid-February weekdays and mid-September through October weekdays (excluding HHN event nights). Summer, spring break, and holidays see the highest attendance. Early mornings before 10 AM consistently offer the shortest wait times.
ticketInfo: Single-park tickets start at $119 for adults and $114 for children. Park-to-park tickets (needed for Hogwarts Express) start at $174. Express Pass from $89/day. Annual passes from $370 to $630.
dining: Three Broomsticks in Hogsmeade is the most popular restaurant with British-style meals and Butterbeer on tap. Mythos Restaurant in The Lost Continent has been voted the best theme park restaurant multiple times. The Burger Digs in Jurassic Park and Cafe 4 in Marvel Super Hero Island offer solid quick-service options.

parkSubtitle: Universal's high-tech water theme park featuring the iconic Krakatau volcano, TapuTapu wearable technology, and virtual queue lines.
parkDescription: Universal's Volcano Bay is a 28-acre water theme park that reimagines the traditional water park experience. Centered around the 200-foot Krakatau volcano, the park features 18 attractions across four themed areas. The TapuTapu wearable technology allows guests to reserve their place in virtual lines rather than standing in physical queues.
history: Universal's Volcano Bay opened on May 25, 2017, replacing the aging Wet 'n Wild water park (which Universal had acquired in 1998). The park was designed as a water theme park rather than a traditional water park, with elaborate theming and storytelling woven throughout. The TapuTapu technology was a first-of-its-kind virtual queue system for water parks.

areas.name: Krakatau
areas.description: The centerpiece of the park - a 200-foot volcano housing the signature Krakatau Aqua Coaster and the dramatic Ko'okiri Body Plunge drop slide.
areas.topRides: Krakatau Aqua Coaster, Ko'okiri Body Plunge

areas.name: Waturi Beach
areas.description: The main beach area featuring a multi-directional wave pool and the park's largest sandy beach.
areas.topRides: Waturi Beach wave pool, The Reef leisure pool

areas.name: Rainforest Village
areas.description: Lush tropical theming with a variety of slide complexes and a winding river.
areas.topRides: Maku and Puhi round raft slides, TeAwa The Fearless River, Taniwha Tubes

areas.name: River Village
areas.description: Family-friendly area with gentler attractions and a kids' play area.
areas.topRides: Kopiko Wai Winding River lazy river, Tot Tiki Reef, Honu of Honu ika Moana

topAttractions.name: Krakatau Aqua Coaster
topAttractions.description: A water coaster using linear induction motor technology to propel four-person canoes uphill through the volcano

topAttractions.name: Ko'okiri Body Plunge
topAttractions.description: A 125-foot, 70-degree drop slide through a trap door - one of the most terrifying water slides in Florida

topAttractions.name: Honu and Honu ika Moana
topAttractions.description: Two multi-person raft slides with massive half-pipe-style walls that send riders soaring

topAttractions.name: Maku and Puhi
topAttractions.description: Two massive round raft slides - one is an open bowl and one is an enclosed tube - both offering intense spinning action

topAttractions.name: TeAwa The Fearless River
topAttractions.description: An intense whitewater rapid-style lazy river - much more thrilling than a typical lazy river experience

insiderTips: TapuTapu wristbands let you tap into a virtual queue and explore the park while waiting - use it strategically for popular slides
Arrive at park opening to experience the most popular slides (Krakatau Aqua Coaster, Ko'okiri Body Plunge) before virtual wait times get long
Premium seating options include private cabanas ($200+) and padded loungers ($30+) - reserve in advance on busy days
The park closes early compared to other Universal parks - plan for a half-day or full-day visit accordingly
Volcano Bay is included in certain Universal annual pass tiers and multi-park tickets
bestTimes: Volcano Bay is least crowded on weekdays during the school year (September through May, excluding holidays). Summer weekends see the highest attendance. The park occasionally closes early or reaches capacity on peak days. Morning hours offer the shortest virtual wait times.
ticketInfo: Single-day tickets start at $80 for adults and $75 for children. Multi-park tickets including Volcano Bay offer savings. Universal annual passes may include Volcano Bay access depending on the tier.
dining: Kohola Reef Restaurant and Social Club is the park's main dining venue with burgers, chicken, and pizza. Whakawaiwai Eats offers tacos and burritos. The Dancing Dragons Boat Bar and Kunuku Boat Bar serve tropical cocktails and frozen drinks right at the water's edge.

parkSubtitle: Universal Orlando's free entertainment, dining, and nightlife district connecting the theme parks - open day and night.
parkDescription: Universal CityWalk is the dining, shopping, and entertainment complex connecting Universal Studios Florida, Islands of Adventure, and Volcano Bay. Spanning the walkway between the parking structures and the theme parks, CityWalk features over 30 restaurants, bars, shops, and entertainment venues. Admission to CityWalk is free.
history: Universal CityWalk opened in 1999 alongside Islands of Adventure as the entertainment hub of Universal Orlando Resort. Modeled after Universal CityWalk in Hollywood, the Orlando version has grown into a premier nightlife and dining destination. The complex has been continuously updated with new restaurants and venues, including the addition of VIVO Italian Kitchen, Toothsome Chocolate Emporium, and Bigfire.

areas.name: Entertainment Row
areas.description: Live music and nightlife venues including Rising Star karaoke, Red Coconut Club, and the groove dance club.
areas.topRides: N/A - entertainment and nightlife

areas.name: Restaurant Row
areas.description: Over 20 dining options ranging from quick-service to upscale table-service restaurants.
areas.topRides: N/A - dining experiences

areas.name: AMC Universal Cineplex
areas.description: A 20-screen movie theater with reclining seats and dine-in options.
areas.topRides: AMC Universal Cineplex 20

topAttractions.name: Toothsome Chocolate Emporium
topAttractions.description: A steampunk-themed restaurant and chocolate factory with towering milkshakes, artisan chocolates, and full dinner menu

topAttractions.name: Bob Marley - A Tribute to Freedom
topAttractions.description: Live reggae music venue recreating Bob Marley's home in Kingston, Jamaica with outdoor courtyard seating

topAttractions.name: Rising Star
topAttractions.description: Interactive karaoke experience where you perform on stage backed by a live band - one of the most fun nightlife spots in Orlando

topAttractions.name: Blue Man Group
topAttractions.description: The iconic Blue Man Group performs at their dedicated theater at CityWalk with high-energy shows combining music, art, and comedy

topAttractions.name: Bigfire
topAttractions.description: Open-fire cooking restaurant serving flame-grilled steaks, seafood, and craft cocktails with a rustic-chic atmosphere

insiderTips: CityWalk is free to enter - no admission required for dining or shopping
Parking is free after 6 PM, making CityWalk an affordable evening out
The CityWalk Party Pass ($13) provides access to all nightclub venues for one night
Toothsome Chocolate Emporium does not take reservations - arrive before 6 PM or after 9 PM to minimize waits
Happy hour deals at multiple restaurants make CityWalk a more affordable dining option than in-park restaurants
The miniature golf courses (Hollywood Drive-In Golf) are open late and fun for groups
bestTimes: CityWalk is busiest on Friday and Saturday nights and during holiday periods. Weekday evenings offer a more relaxed experience. Restaurants are most crowded from 6-8 PM. After 10 PM, the nightlife scene picks up with live music and dancing.
ticketInfo: CityWalk is free to enter. Individual venues have their own pricing. CityWalk Party Pass ($13) covers nightclub admission. Blue Man Group tickets start at $59. Hollywood Drive-In Golf starts at $17. AMC movie tickets at standard pricing.
dining: CityWalk's dining lineup includes Toothsome Chocolate Emporium (steampunk-themed chocolate factory), Bigfire (open-fire grilled cuisine), VIVO Italian Kitchen (fresh pasta), Antojitos Authentic Mexican Food, The Cowfish (burger and sushi fusion), Jimmy Buffett's Margaritaville, and NBC Sports Grill and Brew. Quick-service options include Bread Box Handcrafted Sandwiches, BK Whopper Bar, and Menchie's Frozen Yogurt.

parkSubtitle: Universal's massive new theme park opening in 2025 - featuring worlds themed to Nintendo, Harry Potter, How to Train Your Dragon, and more.
parkDescription: Universal Epic Universe is Universal Orlando's highly anticipated third theme park, opening on May 22, 2025. Located on a separate 750-acre campus south of the existing resort, Epic Universe will be the largest Universal park ever built with five immersive worlds connected by a central celestial hub. The park represents a multi-billion dollar investment and is expected to reshape the Orlando theme park landscape.
history: Universal Epic Universe was announced on August 1, 2019, as Universal Orlando's third theme park. Construction was briefly paused during the COVID-19 pandemic but resumed quickly. The park is being built on land Universal acquired years ago south of Sand Lake Road, separate from the existing resort campus. A new hotel, Universal Helios Grand Hotel, is integrated directly into the park entrance. Epic Universe represents the largest single theme park investment in Florida history.

areas.name: Super Nintendo World
areas.description: A fully immersive recreation of the Mushroom Kingdom from Nintendo's Super Mario games. Interactive wristbands let guests collect coins and compete in activities throughout the land.
areas.topRides: Mario Kart: Bowser's Challenge, Donkey Kong Mine Cart Madness, Yoshi's Adventure

areas.name: The Wizarding World of Harry Potter - Ministry of Magic
areas.description: Set in 1920s Paris from the Fantastic Beasts films, this third Wizarding World land features the French Ministry of Magic and Parisian wizarding street.
areas.topRides: Harry Potter and the Battle at the Ministry

areas.name: How to Train Your Dragon - Isle of Berk
areas.description: An immersive land based on the DreamWorks franchise featuring the Viking village of Berk with live dragons (animatronics) and flying attractions.
areas.topRides: Hiccup's Wing Gliders, Dragon Racer's Rally

areas.name: Dark Universe
areas.description: Based on Universal's classic movie monsters including Dracula, Frankenstein, and the Wolf Man in a gothic, atmospheric world.
areas.topRides: Monsters Unchained: The Frankenstein Experiment

areas.name: Celestial Park
areas.description: The central hub connecting all five worlds with a cosmic observatory theme, gardens, dining, and a spectacular central attraction.
areas.topRides: Stardust Racers dual-launch coaster, Constellation Carousel

topAttractions.name: Mario Kart: Bowser's Challenge
topAttractions.description: AR-enhanced dark ride racing through Mario Kart tracks using augmented reality goggles and physical sets

topAttractions.name: Donkey Kong Mine Cart Madness
topAttractions.description: Family coaster through DK's mine with mine cart vehicles and encounters with Donkey Kong characters

topAttractions.name: Harry Potter and the Battle at the Ministry
topAttractions.description: Next-generation dark ride through the French Ministry of Magic battling dark wizards

topAttractions.name: Monsters Unchained: The Frankenstein Experiment
topAttractions.description: Large-scale dark ride through Dr. Frankenstein's laboratory encountering classic Universal monsters

topAttractions.name: Stardust Racers
topAttractions.description: Dueling launched roller coasters in Celestial Park - two intertwining tracks racing through the cosmic-themed hub

insiderTips: Epic Universe is on a separate campus from the existing Universal parks - plan for travel time between the two campuses
The Helios Grand Hotel connects directly to the park entrance, giving hotel guests early park admission and walkable access
Power-Up Bands in Super Nintendo World ($40) are optional but unlock interactive gameplay throughout the land
Expect very high crowds and long wait times during the opening year - visit on weekdays if possible
Multi-day tickets and new annual pass tiers will include Epic Universe access
The park is expected to add more worlds and attractions in future phases on the massive campus
bestTimes: As a new park opening in 2025, expect extremely high attendance throughout the first year. Weekdays will be less crowded than weekends. January and February traditionally see lower Orlando theme park attendance, but the novelty factor may override normal patterns for the first year.
ticketInfo: Ticket pricing for Epic Universe is expected to start at $129-149 for single-day admission. Multi-park tickets including all three Universal parks will be available. New annual pass tiers will cover Epic Universe. Express Pass availability and pricing to be announced.
dining: Each world within Epic Universe will feature themed dining. Super Nintendo World will include Toadstool Cafe (themed around Princess Peach's restaurant). The Wizarding World area will feature French-inspired magical cuisine. Celestial Park will house the park's largest dining venues. Full dining details will be revealed closer to opening.

parkSubtitle: Marine life theme park featuring world-class roller coasters, animal encounters, and educational experiences in the heart of Orlando.
parkDescription: SeaWorld Orlando is a 200-acre marine zoological theme park that combines world-class roller coasters with animal encounters and educational experiences. The park is home to over 200 species of animals and has evolved to feature some of Florida's most thrilling coasters including Mako, Kraken, Manta, Ice Breaker, and Pipeline: The Surf Coaster.
history: SeaWorld Orlando opened on December 15, 1973, as the second SeaWorld park. The park has transformed significantly over the decades, evolving from primarily an animal show-based park to one of the premier roller coaster destinations in Florida. In 2016, SeaWorld announced the end of its orca breeding program and the phasing out of theatrical orca shows. The park has since invested heavily in new roller coasters and animal rescue programs.

areas.name: Shark Encounter
areas.description: Walk through an underwater tunnel surrounded by sharks, barracuda, and tropical fish in one of the largest underwater viewing tunnels in the world.
areas.topRides: Sharks! exhibit, Shark Encounter

areas.name: Antarctica
areas.description: An immersive area themed to the frozen continent featuring a penguin habitat with over 200 penguins in a climate-controlled environment.
areas.topRides: Antarctica: Empire of the Penguin ride, penguin viewing area

areas.name: Coaster Row
areas.description: SeaWorld's collection of world-class roller coasters spanning the park grounds.
areas.topRides: Mako, Kraken, Manta, Ice Breaker, Pipeline: The Surf Coaster

areas.name: Dolphin Theater Area
areas.description: Home to dolphin presentations, sea lion shows, and interactive animal experiences.
areas.topRides: Dolphin Adventures show, Sea Lion and Otter show

areas.name: Waterfront
areas.description: Scenic lakefront area with dining, paddle boats, and the Infinity Falls river rapids ride.
areas.topRides: Infinity Falls, Journey to Atlantis, Sky Tower

topAttractions.name: Mako
topAttractions.description: Orlando's tallest, fastest, and longest hypercoaster at 200 feet tall and 73 mph - themed after the fastest shark in the ocean

topAttractions.name: Kraken Unleashed
topAttractions.description: Floorless coaster with VR option reaching 65 mph with seven inversions - one of the most intense coasters in Florida

topAttractions.name: Manta
topAttractions.description: Flying coaster where riders are positioned face-down, soaring like a manta ray at 56 mph through pretzel loops

topAttractions.name: Pipeline: The Surf Coaster
topAttractions.description: First-of-its-kind surf coaster where riders stand in a surfing position on a moving platform

topAttractions.name: Ice Breaker
topAttractions.description: Launch coaster with four launches including a beyond-vertical 93-foot spike - the newest coaster at SeaWorld

insiderTips: SeaWorld offers significant discounts when buying tickets online in advance versus at the gate
Quick Queue (SeaWorld's version of Express Pass) is very affordable compared to Universal's Express Pass
The park is far less crowded than Disney or Universal on most days - weekday visits often mean walk-on rides
Sit in the first few rows of Journey to Atlantis and Infinity Falls if you want to get soaked
All-Day Dining Deal ($40) offers unlimited meals at participating restaurants throughout the day
SeaWorld's annual pass is one of the best values in Orlando with free parking and discounts included
bestTimes: SeaWorld Orlando is significantly less crowded than Disney or Universal on most days. Weekdays during the school year offer the shortest waits, with many rides being walk-on. The park is busiest during summer weekends, spring break, and holiday weeks. Special events like Electric Ocean (summer) and SeaWorld's Christmas Celebration add extra entertainment.
ticketInfo: Single-day tickets start at $79 online (higher at the gate). Fun Card (seasonal unlimited visits) from $99. Annual passes from $179 with free parking. Quick Queue starts at $25. Multi-park options with Busch Gardens and Aquatica available.
dining: Voyager's Smokehouse offers excellent BBQ with waterfront seating overlooking the lake. Sharks Underwater Grill is the park's premier dining experience where you eat surrounded by a shark aquarium. Flamecraft Bar serves craft beer and cocktails. Quick-service options include Expedition Cafe and Waterway Grill.

parkSubtitle: SeaWorld's tropical water park blending high-speed water slides with animal encounters and a one-of-a-kind dolphin pass-through lazy river.
parkDescription: Aquatica Orlando is SeaWorld's water park featuring a unique blend of high-speed water slides, lazy rivers, and animal encounters. The park's signature feature is Commerson's Dolphin Pass - a lazy river section that flows through a clear underwater tunnel in a Commerson's dolphin habitat. Aquatica spans 59 acres with over 40 slides, rivers, and lagoons.
history: Aquatica Orlando opened on March 1, 2008, as SeaWorld's first dedicated water park. The park was designed to set itself apart from competitors by incorporating marine life encounters into the water park experience. New slides have been added regularly, including Riptide Race (dual-rider mat racer) in 2020 and KareKare Curl in 2021.

areas.name: Thrill Slides
areas.description: The park's most intense water slides launching from the central tower complex.
areas.topRides: Ihu's Breakaway Falls, Dolphin Plunge, Taumata Racer

areas.name: Dolphin Area
areas.description: The unique section featuring Commerson's dolphin viewing and the pass-through lazy river.
areas.topRides: Commerson's Dolphin Pass, Loggerhead Lane lazy river

areas.name: Wave Pools and Beaches
areas.description: Two massive wave pools with sandy beaches and palm tree-lined shores.
areas.topRides: Big Surf Shores, Cutback Cove

areas.name: Kata's Kookaburra Cove
areas.description: Dedicated kids' area with scaled-down slides and a shallow play pool for younger children.
areas.topRides: Kata's Kookaburra Cove, Walkabout Waters

topAttractions.name: Ihu's Breakaway Falls
topAttractions.description: The tallest, steepest, and only multi-drop tower slide of its kind in Orlando - riders step onto a transparent floor that drops away

topAttractions.name: Dolphin Plunge
topAttractions.description: Enclosed tube slide that passes through an underwater section in a Commerson's dolphin habitat

topAttractions.name: Riptide Race
topAttractions.description: Dual-rider mat racer slide where two riders compete head-to-head down a 68-foot-tall slide tower

topAttractions.name: Taumata Racer
topAttractions.description: Eight-lane mat racer slide where riders race head-first down the slope

topAttractions.name: KareKare Curl
topAttractions.description: Open-air curl slide with a near-vertical wave wall that sends riders weightless at the top

insiderTips: Aquatica is significantly less crowded than Disney water parks and typically offers much shorter waits
Combine an Aquatica visit with SeaWorld using a multi-park ticket for the best value
Ihu's Breakaway Falls is the most thrilling slide - the floor drops out from under you
Free life jackets are available for children at multiple locations throughout the park
The All-Day Dining Deal covers unlimited meals and is well worth it for a full-day visit
bestTimes: Aquatica is least crowded on weekdays during the school year. Summer weekends and holidays see the highest attendance. The park operates year-round but with reduced hours in winter months. Morning arrival ensures the best experience with shorter waits.
ticketInfo: Single-day tickets from $49 online. Combo tickets with SeaWorld from $80. SeaWorld annual passes include Aquatica access at certain tiers. Quick Queue available for purchase.
dining: Banana Beach Cook-Out is an all-you-can-eat buffet option included in some ticket packages. Waterstone Grill serves burgers, chicken, and salads. Mango Market offers quick bites and frozen treats. Multiple bar locations serve tropical cocktails and cold beer throughout the park.

parkSubtitle: An exclusive all-inclusive day resort where you swim with dolphins, snorkel with tropical fish, and relax in a tropical paradise - limited to 1,300 guests per day.
parkDescription: Discovery Cove is SeaWorld's ultra-premium, all-inclusive day resort limited to just 1,300 guests per day. The park offers an intimate tropical paradise experience including swimming with bottlenose dolphins, snorkeling in a coral reef with thousands of tropical fish and rays, floating down a lazy river through an aviary, and relaxing on pristine sandy beaches.
history: Discovery Cove opened on July 25, 2000, as a revolutionary concept in the theme park industry - a premium, reservation-only day resort. The park was designed to provide an exclusive, crowd-free alternative to traditional theme parks. Every guest receives all-inclusive access to food, drinks, gear, and experiences. The Dolphin Swim experience has been the park's signature offering since day one.

areas.name: Dolphin Lagoon
areas.description: The signature experience where guests interact with and swim alongside Atlantic bottlenose dolphins in a 30-minute guided encounter.
areas.topRides: Dolphin Swim Experience

areas.name: Grand Reef
areas.description: A massive snorkeling reef with thousands of tropical fish, rays, and marine life. Optional SeaVenture helmet diving experience available.
areas.topRides: Grand Reef snorkeling, SeaVenture helmet diving

areas.name: Explorer's Aviary
areas.description: A tropical aviary home to over 250 exotic birds where guests can feed and interact with the birds while floating through on the Wind-Away River.
areas.topRides: Wind-Away River through aviary, bird feeding

areas.name: Serenity Bay
areas.description: A white sand beach area with crystal-clear freshwater pools, cabanas, and hammocks for relaxation.
areas.topRides: Freshwater Oasis, Serenity Bay beach

topAttractions.name: Dolphin Swim Experience
topAttractions.description: A 30-minute guided interaction with Atlantic bottlenose dolphins including swimming, hugging, and being towed through the water

topAttractions.name: Grand Reef Snorkeling
topAttractions.description: Snorkel through a massive reef with thousands of tropical fish, stingrays, and even sharks behind a clear partition

topAttractions.name: SeaVenture
topAttractions.description: Walk along the bottom of the Grand Reef in a diving helmet, hand-feeding fish and rays 10 feet underwater

topAttractions.name: Wind-Away River
topAttractions.description: Lazy river winding through tropical landscapes and through the Explorer's Aviary filled with exotic birds

topAttractions.name: Freshwater Oasis
topAttractions.description: Crystal-clear wading pool where guests walk among Asian small-clawed otters and tropical marmosets

insiderTips: Book as far in advance as possible - Discovery Cove sells out regularly, especially during peak seasons
The all-inclusive admission includes breakfast, lunch, snacks, unlimited alcoholic and non-alcoholic drinks, wetsuit, snorkel gear, sunscreen, and towels
Packages include 14 days of unlimited admission to SeaWorld and Aquatica - an incredible bonus value
The Dolphin Swim upgrade adds about $100-150 to the base price but is the signature experience
SeaVenture ($60 add-on) is an unforgettable experience of walking on the reef floor in a diving helmet
Arrive right at 8 AM to maximize your day - the park feels like a private tropical island in the morning
bestTimes: Discovery Cove limits attendance to 1,300 guests per day regardless of the season, so the experience is consistently uncrowded. However, booking availability is harder to find during summer, spring break, and holidays. Weekdays tend to have more availability. The weather is most pleasant from March through May and October through November.
ticketInfo: Day resort packages start at $199 per person (without dolphin swim) and $299+ per person (with dolphin swim). All-inclusive: breakfast, lunch, unlimited drinks (including alcohol), snorkel gear, wetsuit, towels, locker, sunscreen. Includes 14 consecutive days at SeaWorld and Aquatica. Reservations required.
dining: All dining is included in admission. Laguna Grill serves a full hot breakfast and lunch buffet with grilled chicken, fish, burgers, salads, and sides. Freshly baked cookies and snacks are available throughout the day. Unlimited beer, wine, cocktails, and soft drinks are served at bars and kiosks around the park.

parkSubtitle: The ultimate theme park for kids ages 2-12 - featuring over 50 rides, shows, and attractions plus Miniland USA built from millions of LEGO bricks.
parkDescription: LEGOLAND Florida Resort is a 150-acre theme park and water park designed specifically for families with children ages 2-12. Located in Winter Haven about 45 minutes from Orlando, the park features over 50 rides, shows, and attractions, a water park, and Miniland USA - a massive display of American landmarks built from millions of LEGO bricks.
history: LEGOLAND Florida opened on October 15, 2011, on the site of the former Cypress Gardens theme park, which operated from 1936 to 2009. The park preserved and incorporated the historic Cypress Gardens botanical gardens, including the iconic banyan tree and Florida Pool. LEGOLAND has expanded significantly since opening, adding Heartlake City (2015), The LEGO Movie World (2019), and Peppa Pig Theme Park (2022) adjacent to the resort.

areas.name: Miniland USA
areas.description: Incredible miniature recreations of American landmarks including Las Vegas, New York, Washington D.C., and the Kennedy Space Center - all built from millions of LEGO bricks.
areas.topRides: Miniland USA walkthrough

areas.name: The LEGO Movie World
areas.description: Based on The LEGO Movie films, featuring Bricksburg brought to life with a flying ride and character meet-and-greets.
areas.topRides: The LEGO Movie Masters of Flight, Unikitty's Disco Drop, Battle of Bricksburg

areas.name: LEGO Kingdoms
areas.description: Medieval-themed area with the park's signature coaster and a jousting show.
areas.topRides: The Dragon coaster, Royal Joust, Merlin's Challenge

areas.name: LEGO City
areas.description: Kids can drive, fly, and navigate boats in a miniature LEGO City designed for hands-on play.
areas.topRides: Driving School, Flying School, Rescue Academy, Coastersaurus

areas.name: Cypress Gardens
areas.description: The historic botanical gardens preserved from the original Cypress Gardens park, featuring beautiful landscaping, the iconic Florida Pool, and towering banyan trees.
areas.topRides: Cypress Gardens botanical walkthrough

topAttractions.name: The Dragon
topAttractions.description: The park's signature coaster that begins as a dark ride through a LEGO castle and then launches outdoors for a thrilling coaster experience

topAttractions.name: The LEGO Movie Masters of Flight
topAttractions.description: Immersive flying theater ride soaring over LEGO-built worlds from The LEGO Movie

topAttractions.name: Miniland USA
topAttractions.description: Awe-inspiring miniature landmarks built from over 32 million LEGO bricks - including a functional LEGO Kennedy Space Center launch

topAttractions.name: Coastersaurus
topAttractions.description: Wooden roller coaster winding through a life-sized LEGO dinosaur garden - fun for the whole family

topAttractions.name: Build and Test
topAttractions.description: Hands-on building experience where kids construct LEGO vehicles and race them on a test track

insiderTips: LEGOLAND is designed for kids ages 2-12 - teens and adults without children may find the rides too tame
The resort hotel (LEGOLAND Hotel and Pirate Island Hotel) offers themed rooms and early park access
Peppa Pig Theme Park is a separate gate adjacent to LEGOLAND with an additional ticket required
Bring your own LEGO minifigures to trade with park employees - they all carry them for trading
The water park requires a combo ticket and is well worth it on hot days
LEGOLAND is about 45 minutes from Orlando/Kissimmee - plan for the drive time
bestTimes: LEGOLAND is least crowded on weekdays during the school year (September through May, excluding holidays). The park does not operate daily year-round - check the calendar as some weekdays are closed during the off-season. Summer and holiday weeks see the highest attendance.
ticketInfo: Single-day tickets start at $104 for ages 3+ ($99 online). Combo tickets with water park start at $129. Peppa Pig Theme Park is a separate ticket ($34). Annual passes from $199. Multi-day and hotel packages available for savings.
dining: Bricksburg Market Food Hall in LEGO Movie World offers burgers, pizza, and chicken tenders. The Dragon's Den in LEGO Kingdoms serves turkey legs and BBQ. Fun Town Pizza and Pasta Buffet is a popular all-you-can-eat option. Granny's Apple Fries are a must-try park snack - cinnamon-sugar coated apple fries with whipped cream.

parkSubtitle: Orlando's affordable family fun park on International Drive - featuring Florida's only wooden coaster and go-kart tracks with free admission.
parkDescription: Fun Spot America Orlando is a family-owned amusement park on International Drive offering a mix of roller coasters, go-kart tracks, flat rides, and an arcade. The park features free admission with pay-per-ride or all-day wristband options, making it one of the most affordable theme park experiences in Orlando.
history: Fun Spot America began as a small go-kart track operation in 1997 and has grown into a full amusement park with coasters and thrill rides. The park added White Lightning, the only wooden roller coaster in Orlando, in 2013 and Freedom Flyer, a suspended family coaster, in 2014. Fun Spot remains family-owned and operated, distinguishing it from the corporate mega-parks nearby.

areas.name: Coaster Area
areas.description: Home to the park's roller coasters including the signature White Lightning wooden coaster.
areas.topRides: White Lightning, Freedom Flyer, Mine Blower

areas.name: Go-Kart Tracks
areas.description: Multiple multi-level go-kart tracks including the tallest SkyCoaster in the world.
areas.topRides: Quad Helix track, Commander track, Thrasher track

areas.name: Rides and Attractions
areas.description: Flat rides, a Ferris wheel, and family-friendly attractions throughout the park.
areas.topRides: SkyCoaster, Fun Spot Ferris Wheel, Screamer, Rockstar Coaster

topAttractions.name: White Lightning
topAttractions.description: Orlando's only wooden roller coaster - a compact but surprisingly intense ride with steep drops and airtime moments

topAttractions.name: Freedom Flyer
topAttractions.description: Suspended family coaster where riders' feet dangle as they soar through turns and dips

topAttractions.name: SkyCoaster
topAttractions.description: The world's tallest SkyCoaster - a free-fall swing launching riders from 300 feet at speeds up to 80 mph (separate ticket)

topAttractions.name: Go-Kart Tracks
topAttractions.description: Multiple multi-level go-kart tracks including elevated tracks with great views of International Drive

topAttractions.name: Mine Blower
topAttractions.description: Wooden coaster with an inversion - a rarity in wooden coaster design - fast and fun

insiderTips: Admission to the park is free - you only pay for rides via individual tickets or a wristband
The all-day wristband ($55) is the best value if you plan to ride multiple attractions
Fun Spot is open late (until midnight on some nights), making it a great after-dinner activity
The SkyCoaster ($40+) is a separate charge even with a wristband but is an unforgettable experience
The park is right on International Drive, so it is easy to combine with nearby restaurants and attractions
bestTimes: Fun Spot is least crowded on weekday mornings. Weekend evenings and holiday periods see the most visitors. The park is a good rainy-day backup since many rides operate in light rain and you can wait for weather to pass without wasting an expensive park ticket.
ticketInfo: Free admission. Individual ride tickets from $5. All-day ride armband $55 for guests over 54 inches, $45 for under 54 inches. SkyCoaster from $40 (separate charge). Go-kart rides from $9.
dining: Fun Spot offers basic amusement park fare including pizza, burgers, hot dogs, and funnel cakes at the on-site food court. The International Drive location means dozens of restaurants are within walking distance.

parkSubtitle: Kissimmee's family fun park near Disney with the headliner Mine Blower coaster, go-karts, and free admission.
parkDescription: Fun Spot America Kissimmee is the sister park to Fun Spot Orlando, located on US-192 near Walt Disney World. The park features the headliner Mine Blower wooden coaster (one of the few wooden coasters in the world with an inversion), multiple go-kart tracks, flat rides, and an arcade.
history: Fun Spot Kissimmee opened in 2002 as Old Town's adjacent amusement park before evolving into a standalone Fun Spot America location. The park's biggest addition was Mine Blower in 2017, a wooden roller coaster featuring an inversion - a rarity that generates enthusiast attention. The park shares ownership with Fun Spot Orlando but operates independently.

areas.name: Mine Blower Area
areas.description: The headliner coaster area featuring the park's signature wooden roller coaster.
areas.topRides: Mine Blower, Headhunter

areas.name: Go-Kart Zone
areas.description: Multiple go-kart tracks of varying intensity for all ages.
areas.topRides: Vortex track, Chaos track, Road Course

areas.name: Family Rides
areas.description: A collection of flat rides and family-friendly attractions.
areas.topRides: SkyCoaster, Hot Seat, Screamer, Kiddie rides

topAttractions.name: Mine Blower
topAttractions.description: Wooden coaster with an inversion - a rare feat in wooden coaster engineering that draws coaster enthusiasts from around the world

topAttractions.name: Headhunter
topAttractions.description: Spinning coaster that whips riders through tight turns and unexpected spins

topAttractions.name: SkyCoaster
topAttractions.description: Free-fall swing experience launching from a high tower - an adrenaline rush beyond traditional rides

topAttractions.name: Go-Kart Tracks
topAttractions.description: Multiple tracks including single-rider and double-rider karts for families and competitive racers

topAttractions.name: Gator Spot
topAttractions.description: Alligator and wildlife exhibit within the park featuring Florida native animals

insiderTips: Free admission - pay only for the rides you want or buy an all-day wristband
Mine Blower is a hidden gem that coaster enthusiasts specifically seek out
Located right next to Old Town Kissimmee, so you can combine both in one evening
Less crowded than the International Drive Fun Spot location
