Open late on weekends - great for an evening activity after a day at Disney
bestTimes: Fun Spot Kissimmee is least crowded on weekday mornings and early afternoons. The park's proximity to Disney means it sees more traffic on Disney non-park days and evenings. Weekend evenings bring the largest crowds, especially when Old Town hosts car shows.
ticketInfo: Free admission. Individual ride tickets from $5. All-day ride armband $55 over 54 inches, $45 under 54 inches. SkyCoaster from $40 (separate). Go-karts from $9.
dining: Basic amusement park food court with pizza, burgers, and snacks. Old Town Kissimmee next door offers additional restaurant options including A&W, Flippers Pizza, and more.

parkSubtitle: The Alligator Capital of the World - a classic Florida attraction since 1949 with thousands of alligators, zip lines, and the famous gator mouth entrance.
parkDescription: Gatorland is a 110-acre wildlife preserve and theme park known as the Alligator Capital of the World. Home to thousands of alligators and crocodiles, the park has been a Florida institution since 1949. The iconic giant alligator mouth entrance is one of the most photographed landmarks in Orlando. The park offers a more authentic, old-Florida experience compared to the mega theme parks.
history: Gatorland was founded by Owen Godwin in 1949 as the Florida Wildlife Institute, starting with just a handful of alligators and a roadside stand. The iconic giant gator mouth entrance was built in 1962 and has become one of the most recognized landmarks in Orlando. The park survived a devastating fire in 2006 that destroyed the main building but was rebuilt and reopened. Gatorland remains family-owned and operated to this day, now in its third generation.

areas.name: Alligator Breeding Marsh
areas.description: A massive natural marsh boardwalk where guests walk among free-roaming alligators in their natural habitat - one of the most unique experiences in Orlando.
areas.topRides: Breeding Marsh boardwalk, observation tower

areas.name: Screamin' Gator Zip Line
areas.description: A thrilling zip line course that sends riders soaring over alligator-filled ponds and the Cuban crocodile habitat.
areas.topRides: Screamin' Gator Zip Line, Stompin' Gator Off-Road Adventure

areas.name: Shows and Encounters
areas.description: Live shows featuring gator wrestling, gator jumparoo (where alligators leap out of the water for food), and animal encounters.
areas.topRides: Gator Wrestlin' show, Gator Jumparoo, Up Close Encounters

areas.name: Wildlife Discovery
areas.description: Beyond alligators, the park features a petting zoo, aviary, panther habitat, and native Florida wildlife exhibits.
areas.topRides: Petting zoo, free-flight aviary, panther habitat

topAttractions.name: Alligator Breeding Marsh Boardwalk
topAttractions.description: Walk along boardwalks surrounded by hundreds of free-roaming alligators in a natural marsh setting

topAttractions.name: Gator Wrestlin' Show
topAttractions.description: Live gator wrestling demonstration by trained professionals - a classic Florida tradition

topAttractions.name: Screamin' Gator Zip Line
topAttractions.description: Seven zip line stations soaring 65 feet above the park, crossing directly over alligator habitats

topAttractions.name: Gator Jumparoo
topAttractions.description: Watch massive alligators leap several feet out of the water to snatch food - an impressive display of power

topAttractions.name: Trainer for a Day
topAttractions.description: Premium experience where guests work alongside keepers, feeding alligators and learning about reptile care

insiderTips: Gatorland is a fraction of the price of the major theme parks and provides a uniquely Florida experience
The Gator Night Shine (seasonal) lets you see thousands of glowing alligator eyes at night - unforgettable
Trainer for a Day ($130) is an incredible behind-the-scenes experience worth the upgrade
Visit in the morning when alligators are more active and sunning themselves on the banks
The zip line requires a separate ticket ($70+) but is one of the most unique zip line experiences anywhere
Gatorland is between Orlando and Kissimmee on US-441, about 20 minutes from Disney
bestTimes: Gatorland is least crowded on weekday mornings. The park is a hidden gem that even locals often overlook. Spring and fall offer the most comfortable weather for walking the outdoor exhibits. Summer afternoons can be very hot - bring water and sunscreen.
ticketInfo: Adult tickets $29.99, children (3-12) $21.99. Zip line add-on $69.99+. Trainer for a Day $130. Stompin' Gator Off-Road Adventure $12. Annual passes available. Significant online discounts available.
dining: Pearl's Smokehouse serves BBQ, burgers, and gator bites (yes, real alligator meat). Gator Jake's fry stand offers gator nuggets and fried fare. The gift shop sells gator jerky to take home.

parkSubtitle: International Drive's entertainment complex anchored by The Wheel (400 feet), Madame Tussauds, SEA LIFE Aquarium, and Museum of Illusions.
parkDescription: ICON Park is a 20-acre entertainment complex on International Drive featuring The Wheel at ICON Park (a 400-foot observation wheel), Madame Tussauds Orlando, SEA LIFE Orlando Aquarium, Museum of Illusions, and over 50 restaurants and shops. The complex serves as the anchor of the International Drive entertainment corridor.
history: ICON Park opened in 2015 as I-Drive 360, centered around the Orlando Eye observation wheel. The complex rebranded as ICON Park in 2018 and has expanded to include multiple attractions and over 50 dining and retail venues. The Wheel at ICON Park stands 400 feet tall, making it the tallest observation wheel on the East Coast of the United States.

areas.name: The Wheel
areas.description: The 400-foot observation wheel offering panoramic views of Orlando, including the theme parks on clear days. Each climate-controlled capsule holds up to 15 guests.
areas.topRides: The Wheel at ICON Park

areas.name: Attractions Row
areas.description: Multiple ticketed attractions including wax figures, aquarium, and optical illusion exhibits.
areas.topRides: Madame Tussauds, SEA LIFE Aquarium, Museum of Illusions

areas.name: Restaurant Row
areas.description: Over 50 restaurants and bars ranging from casual dining to upscale options with outdoor seating.
areas.topRides: N/A - dining and entertainment

topAttractions.name: The Wheel at ICON Park
topAttractions.description: 400-foot observation wheel with climate-controlled capsules offering 360-degree views of Orlando - the tallest on the US East Coast

topAttractions.name: Madame Tussauds Orlando
topAttractions.description: Interactive wax museum featuring lifelike figures of celebrities, athletes, musicians, and historical figures with themed photo zones

topAttractions.name: SEA LIFE Orlando Aquarium
topAttractions.description: Underwater aquarium with a 360-degree ocean tunnel, touch pools, and thousands of marine creatures from Florida and beyond

topAttractions.name: Museum of Illusions
topAttractions.description: Interactive museum of optical illusions, holograms, and mind-bending exhibits designed for photos and social media

topAttractions.name: Pearl Express Train
topAttractions.description: Scenic train ride around the ICON Park complex - a relaxing way to see the grounds

insiderTips: Combo tickets for multiple ICON Park attractions offer significant savings over individual tickets
The Wheel is most spectacular at sunset and after dark when it is illuminated with LED lights
Free parking in the garage is a major perk - most I-Drive attractions charge for parking
Weekday mornings are the least crowded for the attractions
Many restaurants offer happy hour specials in the late afternoon
The complex is walkable to many other I-Drive attractions
bestTimes: ICON Park is open year-round with attractions busiest on weekends and during holiday periods. Weekday mornings offer the shortest waits for The Wheel, Madame Tussauds, and SEA LIFE. Evening visits are popular for the illuminated Wheel and restaurant scene.
ticketInfo: The Wheel: $28 adults, $23 children. Madame Tussauds: $32 adults, $25 children. SEA LIFE: $28 adults, $23 children. Combo tickets from $52. Museum of Illusions: $25 adults, $20 children.
dining: ICON Park offers a wide range of dining including Tin Roof (live music and Southern cuisine), Tapa Toro (Spanish tapas), Shake Shack, Yard House, and Blake's A Steakhouse. Sugar Factory is popular for over-the-top milkshakes and candy cocktails.

parkSubtitle: The upside-down house on International Drive - an interactive science museum with over 100 hands-on exhibits, ropes course, and laser tag.
parkDescription: WonderWorks Orlando is an interactive science and entertainment attraction housed in a distinctive upside-down building on International Drive. The attraction features over 100 hands-on exhibits across six Wonder Zones, plus a 4D theater, indoor ropes course, and laser tag arena. The inverted building exterior is one of the most photographed landmarks on I-Drive.
history: WonderWorks Orlando opened in 1998 in its iconic upside-down building, becoming an instant landmark on International Drive. The backstory claims the building was a secret laboratory that was struck by a tornado experiment gone wrong, flipping the entire structure upside-down. The attraction has been continuously updated with new exhibits and experiences over the years.

areas.name: Natural Disasters Zone
areas.description: Experience the power of hurricanes, earthquakes, and other natural phenomena through simulation exhibits.
areas.topRides: Hurricane simulator, Earthquake simulator

areas.name: Physical Challenge Zone
areas.description: Test your physical abilities with exhibits that challenge balance, agility, and strength.
areas.topRides: Bed of nails, Bubble lab, Pulley challenge

areas.name: Light and Sound Zone
areas.description: Interactive exhibits exploring light, sound, and sensory perception.
areas.topRides: Giant piano, Light exhibits, Tesla coil

areas.name: Space Discovery Zone
areas.description: Space-themed exhibits including astronaut training simulations and space shuttle experiences.
areas.topRides: Astronaut training gyroscope, Space shuttle simulator

areas.name: Ropes Course and Laser Tag
areas.description: A three-story indoor ropes challenge course and a multi-level laser tag arena.
areas.topRides: Indoor ropes course, Laser tag arena

topAttractions.name: Hurricane Category 1 Simulator
topAttractions.description: Step inside a chamber and experience 74 mph hurricane-force winds

topAttractions.name: Indoor Ropes Course
topAttractions.description: Three-story ropes course with 20+ elements suspended above the museum floor

topAttractions.name: Astronaut Training Gyroscope
topAttractions.description: Spin in a multi-axis trainer used by NASA to prepare astronauts for space flight

topAttractions.name: Laser Tag Arena
topAttractions.description: Multi-level, glow-in-the-dark laser tag arena with an immersive sci-fi theme

topAttractions.name: Earthquake Simulator
topAttractions.description: Stand on a shaking platform to feel what a 5.3 magnitude earthquake feels like

insiderTips: Buy tickets online in advance for the best prices - walk-up prices are significantly higher
The all-access combo ticket (exhibits + ropes course + laser tag) is the best value
WonderWorks is a great rainy-day activity since everything is indoors
Allow 2-3 hours to experience all the exhibits, ropes course, and laser tag
The Outta Control Magic Comedy Dinner Show is held at WonderWorks and is a fun add-on
bestTimes: WonderWorks is busiest during rainy days (when tourists come indoors) and holiday periods. Weekday mornings are the least crowded. The attraction is open late, making it a good evening activity after theme parks close.
ticketInfo: All-access combo (exhibits + ropes course + laser tag) $43.99 adults, $33.99 children. Exhibits only $29.99 adults, $22.99 children. Dinner show add-on from $32. Online discounts available.
dining: The Outta Control Magic Comedy Dinner Show at WonderWorks includes unlimited pizza, salad, beer, wine, and soft drinks with a 90-minute comedy and magic performance. Otherwise, the attraction is surrounded by International Drive restaurants within walking distance.

parkSubtitle: Tampa Bay's African-themed mega park - home to world-class roller coasters, a Serengeti Safari, and over 12,000 animals.
parkDescription: Busch Gardens Tampa Bay is a 335-acre African-themed theme park and zoo that combines world-class roller coasters with a world-class zoological experience. The park is home to over 12,000 animals representing more than 300 species and features some of the most thrilling roller coasters in the Southeast United States, including SheiKra, Cheetah Hunt, Iron Gwazi, and Montu.
history: Busch Gardens began as a hospitality house and bird garden for the Anheuser-Busch brewery in Tampa, opening to the public in 1959. The park evolved through the decades from a small attraction to a major theme park, adding African theming and significant animal collections in the 1970s and world-class roller coasters from the 1990s onward. Now owned by SeaWorld Entertainment, the park continues to add major coasters, with Iron Gwazi (2022) being the world's fastest and steepest hybrid coaster at its opening.

areas.name: Serengeti Plain
areas.description: A 65-acre open-air habitat where African animals roam freely, visible from the Serengeti Railway, Skyride, and the Serengeti Safari tour.
areas.topRides: Serengeti Safari tour, Serengeti Railway, Cheetah Hunt

areas.name: Iron Gwazi Area
areas.description: Home to the park's newest and most extreme roller coaster.
areas.topRides: Iron Gwazi - the world's fastest and steepest hybrid coaster at opening

areas.name: Egypt
areas.description: Ancient Egyptian theming surrounding one of the most acclaimed inverted coasters ever built.
areas.topRides: Montu, King Tut's Tomb replica

areas.name: Nairobi
areas.description: Animal exhibits and encounters including the Myombe Reserve great ape habitat and the Edge of Africa walking trail.
areas.topRides: Myombe Reserve, Edge of Africa, Nairobi Station

areas.name: Pantopia
areas.description: A vibrant cultural area featuring the Falcon's Fury drop tower and Cheetah Hunt coaster launch point.
areas.topRides: Falcon's Fury, Cheetah Hunt, Cobra's Curse

areas.name: Congo
areas.description: Tropical river theming with water rides and the historic Kumba coaster.
areas.topRides: Kumba, Congo River Rapids, Stanley Falls Flume

topAttractions.name: Iron Gwazi
topAttractions.description: World-class hybrid coaster - 206 feet tall, 76 mph, 12 airtime moments, and a beyond-vertical 91-degree drop

topAttractions.name: Cheetah Hunt
topAttractions.description: Triple-launch coaster inspired by the cheetah's hunting sprint, reaching 60 mph along a course that crosses the Serengeti edge

topAttractions.name: Montu
topAttractions.description: Legendary inverted coaster considered one of the best in the world with seven inversions at 60 mph

topAttractions.name: SheiKra
topAttractions.description: Floorless dive coaster with a 90-degree vertical drop from 200 feet and a water splash finale

topAttractions.name: Serengeti Safari
topAttractions.description: Guided open-air truck tour across the 65-acre Serengeti Plain with opportunities to hand-feed giraffes

insiderTips: Busch Gardens is about 75 minutes from Orlando - factor in the drive time when planning your day
Iron Gwazi and Cheetah Hunt have the longest waits - ride them first at rope drop
The Serengeti Safari ($39) is one of the best animal experiences in Florida and books up fast
Quick Queue is very affordable here compared to Universal's Express Pass and dramatically reduces wait times
Busch Gardens annual passes often include free admission to SeaWorld and Aquatica
The park is significantly less crowded than Orlando theme parks on most days
bestTimes: Busch Gardens Tampa is significantly less crowded than Orlando's major theme parks. Weekdays during the school year offer walk-on rides for most coasters. The park is busiest during summer weekends, spring break, and the Howl-O-Scream Halloween event. Morning hours consistently offer the shortest wait times.
ticketInfo: Single-day tickets from $109 online (higher at gate). Annual passes from $199 (often include SeaWorld and Aquatica). Quick Queue from $25. Multi-park combos with SeaWorld available. Serengeti Safari tour $39+.
dining: Dragon Fire Grill features live entertainment with flatbreads, burgers, and craft beer. Zambia Smokehouse serves excellent BBQ ribs and chicken. Serengeti Overlook Restaurant provides views of the Serengeti Plain. The Cheetah Run Cafe is a convenient quick-service option near Cheetah Hunt.

parkSubtitle: Busch Gardens' tropical water park in Tampa - featuring high-speed slides, a massive wave pool, and a relaxing lazy river.
parkDescription: Adventure Island is a 30-acre water park located adjacent to Busch Gardens Tampa Bay. The park features a tropical Key West theme with water slides, a wave pool, lazy river, and children's play areas. It operates as a seasonal complement to Busch Gardens, offering guests a way to cool off during Florida's hot summer months.
parkingInfo: $25/day, free with Busch Gardens annual pass
history: Adventure Island opened in 1980 as a companion water park to Busch Gardens Tampa. Originally operated by Anheuser-Busch, the park is now part of the SeaWorld Entertainment portfolio. The park has undergone periodic updates with new slides and renovations, including the addition of Vanish Point and Solar Vortex in recent years.

areas.name: Thrill Slides
areas.description: High-speed water slides launching from the park's central tower.
areas.topRides: Vanish Point, Colossal Curl, Solar Vortex

areas.name: Wave Pool and Beach
areas.description: A large wave pool with sandy beach area for relaxation.
areas.topRides: Endless Surf wave pool, sandy beach

areas.name: Family Zone
areas.description: Family-friendly slides, lazy river, and children's play areas.
areas.topRides: Rambling Bayou lazy river, Splash Attack kiddie area, Fabian's Funport

topAttractions.name: Vanish Point
topAttractions.description: Three drop slides using trap-door launch capsules - riders stand on a floor that drops away, sending them plummeting down enclosed tubes

topAttractions.name: Colossal Curl
topAttractions.description: Family raft slide with a massive wave wall that sends riders soaring on a vertical half-pipe

topAttractions.name: Solar Vortex
topAttractions.description: Florida's first dual-tailspin water slide with enclosed funnel elements

topAttractions.name: Rambling Bayou
topAttractions.description: Scenic lazy river winding through tropical landscaping - the most relaxing experience in the park

topAttractions.name: Endless Surf
topAttractions.description: Large wave pool with rolling waves and a sandy beach shoreline

insiderTips: Adventure Island is right next to Busch Gardens - easy to do both parks in a two-day visit
The park operates seasonally (roughly March through October) - check the calendar
Busch Gardens annual pass holders often get free or discounted Adventure Island access
Arrive at opening on hot days - the park can reach capacity during summer weekends
Cabana rentals include shade, chairs, a locker, and food/drink delivery service
bestTimes: Adventure Island is least crowded on weekday mornings outside of summer school breaks. The park operates seasonally, generally from March through October. Summer weekends see the highest attendance. Morning arrival is recommended to claim a good spot and minimize slide waits.
ticketInfo: Single-day tickets from $49 online. Combo tickets with Busch Gardens available. Busch Gardens annual pass may include Adventure Island access. Cabana rentals from $150+.
dining: Mango Joe's Cafe serves burgers, chicken, and tropical-themed items. Surfside Sandwiches offers subs and wraps. Multiple bar locations serve frozen cocktails and cold beer.

parkSubtitle: Voted the number one zoo in the US by multiple publications - home to the Florida Manatee rehabilitation center and African safari experiences.
parkDescription: ZooTampa at Lowry Park is a 63-acre nonprofit zoo that has been consistently ranked among the top zoos in the United States. The zoo is home to over 1,300 animals representing more than 200 species and operates a critical Florida Manatee rehabilitation center. The zoo is known for its lush tropical setting, immersive habitats, and conservation-focused mission.
history: ZooTampa (formerly Lowry Park Zoo) has served the Tampa Bay community since 1957. The zoo underwent a major transformation in the 2000s and 2010s, earning recognition as the number one zoo in the US from publications including Yelp, TripAdvisor, and Parents Magazine. The zoo's David A. Straz Jr. Manatee Critical Care Center is one of only three facilities in Florida licensed to care for injured manatees.

The zoo's Creatures of the Night (Halloween) and Christmas in the Wild events are popular seasonal experiences
bestTimes: ZooTampa is least crowded on weekday mornings during the school year. The zoo is busiest on weekends, holidays, and during school field trip season (spring). Animals are most active in the morning hours and on cooler days. Summer afternoons see the lowest attendance but also the hottest temperatures.

dining: Safari Cafe offers burgers, salads, and sandwiches in a covered pavilion. Kat's Kitchen serves pizza and chicken tenders. The Canopy Cafe near Africa features African-inspired dishes. Multiple snack stands offer ice cream, churros, and cold drinks.

parkSubtitle: Tampa's premier aquarium on the downtown waterfront - home to over 7,000 aquatic animals, a coral reef exhibit, and wild dolphin eco-tours.
parkDescription: The Florida Aquarium is a 250,000-square-foot aquarium located on the Tampa waterfront in the Channel District. Home to over 7,000 aquatic plants and animals, the aquarium traces Florida's water journey from a freshwater spring to the open ocean. The aquarium is known for its coral reef restoration program and wild dolphin eco-cruise tours.
history: The Florida Aquarium opened on March 31, 1995, in Tampa's Channel District. The aquarium was designed to tell the story of water in Florida, tracing the journey from underground springs through wetlands and bays to the coral reefs of the Gulf of Mexico. The aquarium has been a leader in coral reef restoration, successfully breeding Atlantic coral species in its labs to help restore damaged reefs.

areas.topRides: Wetlands walkthrough, otter viewing

areas.description: African penguin colony in a climate-controlled habitat.
areas.topRides: Penguin viewing, penguin encounter (ticketed)

areas.topRides: Outdoor splash pad, touch tanks

topAttractions.description: A 500,000-gallon tank with floor-to-ceiling viewing windows showing sharks, sea turtles, rays, and a living coral reef

Arrive when the aquarium opens at 9:30 AM for the least crowded experience and best animal activity

The aquarium is on the Tampa waterfront near the cruise port - easy to combine with a pre-cruise visit
Penguin Backstage Pass sells out quickly during peak periods - book in advance
bestTimes: The Florida Aquarium is least crowded on weekday mornings during the school year. Rainy days tend to be busier as visitors seek indoor activities. Summer and holiday periods see the highest attendance. Allow 2-3 hours for the full aquarium experience plus additional time for eco-cruise or animal encounters.
ticketInfo: Adult tickets $34.95, children (3-11) $29.95, seniors $31.95. Wild Dolphin Eco-Cruise $29.95 add-on. Penguin Backstage Pass $30+. Swim with the Fishes from $175. Annual memberships from $90.
dining: Cafe Ray serves lunch items including sandwiches, salads, and kids' meals. The 4D Theater snack bar offers popcorn and drinks. The aquarium is located in Tampa's Channel District with numerous restaurants within walking distance.

parkSubtitle: Home of Winter the Dolphin from Dolphin Tale - a working marine rescue and rehabilitation hospital on Clearwater Beach.
parkDescription: Clearwater Marine Aquarium is a marine animal rescue center and aquarium located on Clearwater Beach. The facility gained international fame as the home of Winter the dolphin, star of the Dolphin Tale movies. Unlike traditional aquariums, CMA functions as a working hospital where guests can see rescued animals being rehabilitated for release back into the wild.
history: Clearwater Marine Aquarium was founded in 1972 as the Clearwater Marine Science Center. The facility transformed from a small local aquarium into a major tourist destination after rescuing Winter the dolphin in 2005. Winter lost her tail and was fitted with a prosthetic, inspiring the Dolphin Tale movie (2011) starring Morgan Freeman. The aquarium completed a $90 million expansion in 2020, tripling its size. Winter passed away in 2021 but her legacy of rescue and rehabilitation continues.

topAttractions.name: Sea Turtle Rehabilitation

topAttractions.name: Otter Exhibit

The boat tour into Clearwater Harbor offers excellent wild dolphin sighting rates
Arrival at opening (10 AM) provides the best experience before school groups and afternoon crowds arrive
The aquarium is on the Clearwater Beach causeway, easily combined with a beach day
Behind-the-scenes tours offer closer animal encounters and support the rescue mission
bestTimes: Clearwater Marine Aquarium is least crowded on weekday mornings during the school year. The facility is busiest during summer vacation, spring break, and holiday weeks. Allow 2-3 hours for the full aquarium experience and additional time for boat tours.

dining: The aquarium cafe offers sandwiches, snacks, and beverages. Clearwater Beach is steps away with dozens of waterfront restaurants including Frenchy's, Cooters, and the Beachcomber.

parkSubtitle: Daytona Beach's waterpark and family entertainment center with water slides, go-karts, laser tag, and mini golf.
parkDescription: Daytona Lagoon is a family entertainment center and water park located in Daytona Beach. The facility combines a water park with go-karts, laser tag, miniature golf, an arcade, and a rock climbing wall. It serves as one of the main family attractions in the Daytona Beach area beyond the beach and speedway.
history: Daytona Lagoon opened in 2005 as a replacement for a previous water park on the site. The facility was designed to serve both the local Daytona Beach community and the tourist market. The park has been periodically updated with new slides and dry-side entertainment options.

bestTimes: Daytona Lagoon is least crowded on weekday mornings. The water park portion is seasonal (roughly April through September). Summer weekends and Bike Week/Spring Break periods see the highest attendance in Daytona Beach.
ticketInfo: Water park admission from $35 adults, $30 children. Combo passes from $45. Individual dry activities from $8-15 each. Group rates available.
dining: On-site concessions include burgers, pizza, hot dogs, and snacks. The park is located near Daytona Beach's restaurant district with many options nearby.

parkSubtitle: The World Center of Racing - home of the Daytona 500, featuring behind-the-scenes tours, the DAYTONA 500 Experience museum, and race-day excitement.
parkDescription: Daytona International Speedway is one of the most iconic motorsports venues in the world, home to the Daytona 500 - NASCAR's most prestigious race. Beyond race days, the speedway offers year-round experiences including the DAYTONA 500 Experience interactive museum, behind-the-scenes speedway tours, and the Richard Petty Driving Experience where visitors can drive a stock car on the famous 2.5-mile tri-oval.
parkingInfo: Free for tours, varies for events
history: Daytona International Speedway opened in 1959, replacing the legendary Daytona Beach Road Course that ran partially on the beach. Built by NASCAR founder Bill France Sr., the 2.5-mile tri-oval speedway with its signature 31-degree banking has hosted the Daytona 500 every February since 1959. The speedway completed a $400 million renovation called DAYTONA Rising in 2016, adding five expanded and reimagined entrances and modern amenities while preserving the track's racing heritage.

Daytona 500 tickets go on sale a year in advance and premium seats sell out quickly
The DAYTONA Rising renovation made the speedway accessible and comfortable for non-racing fans too
Combine a speedway tour with a visit to the nearby Daytona Beach boardwalk and beach
bestTimes: The speedway offers tours year-round except during major race event weekends. The Daytona 500 (February) is the marquee event but tickets and hotels sell out well in advance. The Coke Zero Sugar 400 (August) and Rolex 24 (January) are other major events. Off-race periods offer the most relaxed tour experience.
ticketInfo: Speedway tours from $25 adults, $20 children. DAYTONA 500 Experience museum from $29. Richard Petty Driving Experience from $199 (ride-along) to $549 (driving experience). Daytona 500 tickets from $75-500+ depending on seating.
dining: During race events, the speedway offers extensive concessions throughout the facility. During non-event tour days, the DAYTONA 500 Experience has a small cafe. The speedway is located near Daytona Beach's restaurant scene on International Speedway Boulevard.

parkSubtitle: A National Historic Landmark featuring the stunning 205-foot Singing Tower carillon, lush gardens, and one of the highest points in peninsular Florida.
parkDescription: Bok Tower Gardens is a 250-acre garden and bird sanctuary in Lake Wales, about an hour south of Orlando. The centerpiece is the 205-foot neo-Gothic and Art Deco Singing Tower, which houses a 60-bell carillon that performs daily concerts. The gardens sit atop Iron Mountain, one of the highest points in peninsular Florida at 298 feet above sea level.
history: Bok Tower Gardens was created by Dutch-American author and Pulitzer Prize winner Edward W. Bok as a gift to the American people. The gardens and tower were dedicated by President Calvin Coolidge on February 1, 1929. Landscape architect Frederick Law Olmsted Jr. (son of the designer of Central Park) designed the gardens. The Singing Tower carillon was built by renowned sculptor Lee Lawrie. The property was designated a National Historic Landmark in 1972.

insiderTips: Time your visit to arrive before the 1 PM or 3 PM carillon concert - the music echoing through the gardens is magical

Bring comfortable walking shoes - the gardens cover 250 acres of gentle hills and paths
Pinewood Estate tours are available seasonally (primarily fall and winter holidays) and require a separate ticket
The Hammock Hollow Children's Garden is a wonderful play area for young children
bestTimes: Bok Tower Gardens is never truly crowded, making it a peaceful escape any time. Spring (February through April) offers the most colorful blooms. Winter holiday season brings festive decorations at Pinewood Estate. Summer afternoons can be very hot - morning visits are most comfortable.

parkSubtitle: The only place in the United States where you can legally swim with wild manatees - a once-in-a-lifetime Florida experience.
parkDescription: Crystal River on Florida's Gulf Coast is the only place in the United States where swimming with wild manatees is legally permitted. The crystal-clear 72-degree springs of Kings Bay and Three Sisters Springs attract hundreds of West Indian manatees during the cooler months (November through March), and multiple licensed tour operators offer guided snorkeling tours to swim alongside these gentle giants.
history: Crystal River has been a manatee haven for centuries, thanks to the constant 72-degree spring water that attracts manatees seeking warmth during winter months. The area was designated as the Crystal River National Wildlife Refuge in 1983 to protect the manatee habitat. Swimming with manatees has been regulated and permitted under strict guidelines to ensure the animals are not harassed. Three Sisters Springs, a key gathering spot, was acquired by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service in 2010.

insiderTips: Peak manatee season is November through March when hundreds of manatees seek warm spring water
Book sunrise tours (6-7 AM departure) for the best experience - fewer boats, calmer water, and more active manatees
Crystal River is about 90 minutes northwest of Orlando - an easy day trip
You must follow strict guidelines - no chasing, grabbing, or actively approaching manatees, but they often come to you
Water is a constant 72 degrees year-round - a wetsuit is recommended even in summer for extended snorkeling
Three Sisters Springs boardwalk is free to walk and offers incredible views even without getting in the water
bestTimes: Peak manatee season runs from mid-November through mid-March, with the highest concentrations during cold snaps when manatees seek warm spring water. January and February typically offer the most manatees. Summer months have fewer manatees but the springs are still beautiful for kayaking and snorkeling. Book tours well in advance during peak season.
ticketInfo: Guided manatee snorkeling tours from $35-75 per person (includes wetsuit, snorkel gear, and guide). Kayak tours from $40-65. Three Sisters Springs entry by boat $20 (seasonal). Boardwalk viewing is free. Multiple tour operators compete on price - book online for best rates.
dining: Crystal River's waterfront offers several restaurants including The Plantation on Crystal River (upscale seafood), Crackers Bar and Grill (casual waterfront dining), and The Stumpknockers (Southern-style comfort food). The town is small but has solid dining options concentrated along US-19.

parkSubtitle: Florida's legendary underwater mermaid show - a roadside attraction since 1947, now a state park with crystal-clear spring swimming and kayaking.
parkDescription: Weeki Wachee Springs State Park is home to Florida's world-famous live mermaid show, where performers have been performing underwater in the crystal-clear spring since 1947. The park also features Buccaneer Bay water park, kayaking and paddleboarding on the Weeki Wachee River, riverboat cruises, and a wildlife show. The spring pumps 117 million gallons of fresh water daily at a constant 74 degrees.
history: Weeki Wachee Springs was developed as a roadside attraction in 1947 by former US Navy frogman Newton Perry, who trained young women to perform synchronized swimming acts while breathing through underwater air hoses. The mermaid show became a beloved Florida institution and a national sensation, featured in media from Life Magazine to The Simpsons. The attraction was acquired by the state of Florida and became a state park in 2008, preserving this unique piece of Florida history.

Bring water shoes for the rocky spring bottom at Buccaneer Bay

bestTimes: Weeki Wachee Springs is busiest on summer weekends and holidays when the park can reach capacity by mid-morning. Weekdays during the school year offer the most relaxed experience. The park is open year-round. Spring and fall offer comfortable temperatures and smaller crowds.
ticketInfo: Adults (13+) $13, children (6-12) $8, under 6 free. Kayak rentals from $25. Paddleboard rentals available. All shows and Buccaneer Bay swimming included in park admission. Florida residents receive discounted pricing.
dining: The park has a small concession stand serving burgers, hot dogs, and snacks. For more dining options, Weeki Wachee is near Spring Hill and Brooksville with restaurants along US-19.

parkSubtitle: An epic dinner tournament experience - jousting knights, sword fights, and a four-course feast eaten with your hands in a massive castle arena.
parkDescription: Medieval Times Dinner and Tournament in Kissimmee is a two-hour dinner show experience set inside a massive castle arena. Guests are assigned a knight to cheer for as they watch live jousting, sword fighting, and equestrian performances while eating a four-course meal with their hands (no utensils, as was the medieval custom). The 72,000-square-foot castle seats over 1,000 guests per show.
parkHours: Shows typically at 6 PM and 8 PM (varies)
history: Medieval Times opened its Kissimmee castle in 1988 as one of the company's flagship locations. The show concept originated in Spain in the 1970s and was brought to North America in 1983. The Orlando-area castle has been one of the most popular dinner show attractions in Central Florida for over three decades, adapting and updating its show while maintaining the core tournament format.

Kids love this show - cheering for your knight and eating with your hands makes it incredibly fun for families
bestTimes: Medieval Times runs shows year-round with schedules varying by season. Summer and holidays see the most showings per day. Weekday shows tend to be less crowded than Friday and Saturday performances. During peak season, booking a week or more in advance is recommended.
ticketInfo: General admission from $36.95 per person (online advance purchase). Royalty Package (priority seating, commemorative program) from $52.95. Children's pricing available. Group rates for 15+. Birthday and celebration packages available.
dining: The four-course meal is included in admission: garlic bread, tomato bisque soup, roasted chicken (half chicken per guest), corn on the cob, herb-roasted potato, and a dessert pastry. Coffee, tea, and two rounds of soft drinks included. Beer, wine, and cocktails available for purchase. A vegetarian option is available upon request.

parkSubtitle: A swashbuckling dinner show featuring acrobatics, stunts, and musical performances aboard a full-sized pirate ship in a massive indoor lagoon.
parkDescription: Pirate's Dinner Adventure is an interactive dinner show set aboard a full-sized 46-foot pirate ship anchored in a 300,000-gallon indoor lagoon. The show features acrobatic stunts, aerial acts, sword fights, comedy, and musical performances while guests enjoy a three-course meal. After the main show, the party continues with a Buccaneer Bash dance party.
parkHours: Shows typically at 6 PM or 7:30 PM (varies)
history: Pirate's Dinner Adventure opened in Orlando in 2003 on International Drive, later relocating to its current location near the convention center. The show has been updated multiple times over the years but maintains its core formula of pirate-themed entertainment, audience participation, and a multi-course dinner served during the performance.

parkSubtitle: A 1930s gangster-themed dinner show in Kissimmee with comedy, music, and unlimited Italian-American buffet and drinks.
parkDescription: Capone's Dinner and Show is a 1930s-era gangster-themed dinner theater in Kissimmee. The interactive comedy show is set in a prohibition-era speakeasy where the audience gets caught up in a mob feud. The experience includes an unlimited Italian-American buffet and unlimited beer, wine, cocktails, and soft drinks - making it one of the best value dinner shows in the Orlando area.
history: Capone's Dinner and Show has been entertaining guests in Kissimmee since 1995. The locally-owned show has built a loyal following thanks to its combination of interactive comedy, unlimited food and drinks, and affordable pricing. The show has been updated over the years while maintaining its core prohibition-era gangster theme.

parkSubtitle: Universal CityWalk's themed mini golf with two 18-hole courses - The Haunting of Ghostly Greens and Invaders from Planet Putt.
parkDescription: Hollywood Drive-In Golf at Universal CityWalk features two elaborately themed 18-hole miniature golf courses inspired by classic drive-in movie genres. The Haunting of Ghostly Greens is a spooky horror-themed course, while Invaders from Planet Putt takes players through a retro sci-fi alien invasion. Both courses feature special effects, animatronics, and creative hole designs.
parkingInfo: Universal CityWalk parking ($30, free after 6 PM)
history: Hollywood Drive-In Golf opened in 2012 as part of Universal CityWalk Orlando. The courses were designed to be among the most elaborately themed miniature golf experiences in the world, fitting Universal's reputation for immersive entertainment. The late-night hours (until 2 AM) make it one of the few mini golf options available after the theme parks close.

dining: No food on the course, but Universal CityWalk's 30+ restaurants and bars are steps away. Grab dinner before or after your round at any CityWalk venue.

parkSubtitle: African rainforest-themed mini golf with live alligators, caves, waterfalls, and gem mining at multiple Orlando area locations.
parkDescription: Congo River Golf is a chain of African rainforest-themed miniature golf courses with multiple locations in the Orlando area (International Drive, Kissimmee, and Altamonte Springs). Each location features 36 holes of themed mini golf winding through caves, past waterfalls, and alongside live alligator habitats. Additional activities include gem mining and arcade games.
history: Congo River Golf has been a Central Florida mini golf institution since 1993. The courses were designed with elaborate African explorer and rainforest theming, setting them apart from standard mini golf courses. The chain maintains multiple locations throughout the Orlando area, each featuring live alligators as a unique draw.

parkSubtitle: Orlando's I-Drive is the mini golf capital of the world - a comprehensive guide to every course along the famous tourist corridor.
parkDescription: International Drive in Orlando is home to the highest concentration of themed miniature golf courses in the world. Within a few miles, visitors can play courses themed around volcanoes, pirates, dinosaurs, aliens, and more. This guide covers every mini golf option along I-Drive, from budget-friendly community courses to elaborately themed experiences.
history: International Drive has been Orlando's primary tourist corridor since the 1970s, and mini golf courses have been a fixture since the early days. The concentration of themed courses has grown over decades, making the stretch one of the most recognizable collections of miniature golf in the world. From simple putting courses to multi-million dollar themed experiences, I-Drive offers something for every budget.

ticketInfo: Prices range from $10-18 per 18-hole round depending on the course and theming level. Hollywood Drive-In Golf starts at $17.99. Congo River Golf from $14.99. Budget courses from $10-12. Most offer combo discounts for 36 holes.
dining: International Drive is one of Orlando's top dining corridors with hundreds of restaurants within walking distance of every mini golf course. Options range from fast food to upscale dining.

parkSubtitle: A guide to the best go-kart experiences in Orlando - multi-level tracks, competitive racing, and family-friendly options.
parkDescription: The Orlando area is home to some of the best go-kart experiences in Florida, with Fun Spot America's two locations leading the way. Fun Spot Orlando on International Drive and Fun Spot Kissimmee on US-192 both feature multi-level elevated go-kart tracks that are among the most thrilling non-theme-park driving experiences in Central Florida.
parkingInfo: Free at all Fun Spot locations
history: Go-kart racing has been a fixture of Orlando's tourist corridor since the 1970s. Fun Spot America grew from a small go-kart operation into a full amusement park, but go-karts remain the heart of the experience. The multi-level elevated tracks at Fun Spot are some of the tallest and most exciting go-kart tracks in the southeastern United States.

parkSubtitle: A 125,000 square foot indoor entertainment complex on I-Drive featuring high-speed electric go-karts, bowling, zip line, ropes course, and arcade.
parkDescription: Andretti Indoor Karting and Games is a 125,000-square-foot indoor entertainment complex on International Drive. The facility features high-speed electric go-karts on a multi-level indoor track, a ropes course, zip line, bowling, full-motion racing simulators, and an extensive arcade. The venue also includes a full restaurant and bar.
history: Andretti Indoor Karting and Games opened in 2017, bringing the Andretti racing family name to Orlando's International Drive. The facility was designed as a premium indoor entertainment experience, differentiating itself from budget go-kart operations with high-speed Italian-made electric karts, elaborate track design, and a full suite of entertainment options under one roof.
